BSS-GH-gear-type-CPUE-model

Matt George

2026-07-09

###############################################################################
# Crab Creel Estimation - Westport / Grays Harbor
# 2024-25 Season | Framework v5.4
#
# Three population components estimated independently:
#   1. Shore crabbers (dock + jetty + beach) — BSS + PE
#   2. Private boat crabbers — BSS + PE
#   3. Commercial/charter boats — Census/tally
#
# Two gear-regime sub-seasons:
#   Sub-season 1: Ring-net only (Sep 16 – Nov 30, no pots)
#   Sub-season 2: All-gear (Dec 1 – Sep 15, pots allowed)
#
# Port total = sum of all components
#
# v5.4 CHANGES (from v5.3): R-hat convergence gate tightened from 1.05 to 1.01
# (Vehtari et al. 2021), in step with the pooled track. See documentation
# Section 13.
#
# v5.3 CHANGES (from v5.2): align R-side data prep with the gear-hours
# Stan formulation (crab_bss_gear_resolved.stan).
#
#   - For boat fits, the Stan model now expects:
#       * Gear_A_boat[IntA_trailer]  : observed gear per boat group
#                                       (replaces T_A_int, A_A_trailer)
#       * h                          : gear-hours, not crabber-hours
#       * L[d] = 24                  : pots fish 24/7 while trailer is at ramp
#     The previous (v5.2) data list still passed the obsolete T_A_int /
#     A_A_trailer variables and used crabber-hours / day_length for boats,
#     producing the runtime error "Gear_A_boat[IntA_trailer] is missing"
#     and (if that were patched alone) a ~2x downward bias in boat catch.
#
#   - prep_bss_crab() now branches by is_shore for L, h, and the gear-
#     expansion block. is_shore is computed earlier in the function so
#     it can gate the boat-specific gear_time_total filter on int_d.
#
#   - Boat interviews lacking a valid gear_time_total are dropped before
#     the Stan data list is built (analogous to the existing
#     fishing_time_total filter for shore).
#
# v5.2 CHANGES (from v5.1):
#   - Stan model v3.2: Expected value for C_gear (no Poisson noise)
#     for harvest estimates. Separate C_gear_pred with Poisson draws
#     retained for prediction intervals.
#   - Dirichlet-sampled pi_gear in generated quantities propagates
#     gear-proportion uncertainty into C_sum_gear and C_sum. Raw
#     weighted counts (n_weighted_gear) passed from R as data.
#   - Incomplete trip filter: crabbers still fishing at interview
#     have systematically lower CPUE (-20% for pots/traps). Controlled
#     by filter_incomplete_trips parameter (default TRUE).
#   - Divergent transitions now included in convergence pass/fail
#     criterion (max_divergences parameter, default 0).
#   - Trip completion sensitivity analysis added as output.
#   - Incomplete trip statistics reported in diagnostics.
#
# v5.1 CHANGES (from v5.0):
#   - Stan model v3.1: Weighted gear-type CPUE likelihood
#     Multi-gear interviews contribute proportionally to all reported
#     gear types (equal split), replacing the Pot > Ring Net > Trap > Snare
#     priority hierarchy
#   - pi_gear is now DATA (empirical proportions with Laplace smoothing),
#     not a Dirichlet parameter. Categorical likelihood removed.
#   - pi_gear varies by period × day_type (weekday/weekend/holiday)
#   - Per-gear-type process error SD (sigma_eps_C_gear[G_gear])
#   - R_G conditionally estimated (shore) or fixed at 1.3 (boat)
#   - Cauchy priors tightened from scale=5 to scale=2
#   - bss_max_interviews removed (use full interview dataset)
#   - Biweekly BSS periods for ring-net sub-season
#   - Extended convergence checks (C_sum_gear, r_C_gear, sigma_eps_C_gear)
#   - Backward-compatible C[S][D,G] replaced with C_total[D]
#   - Word-boundary regex for gear-type classification
#   - Day length cap raised to 17h for summer accuracy at 46.9°N
###############################################################################

# ===========================================================================
# 0. SETUP — Load packages and configure environment
# ===========================================================================

load.lib <- c("tidyverse","lubridate","suncalc","gt","patchwork","rstan","here")
install.lib <- load.lib[!load.lib %in% installed.packages()]
for(lib in install.lib) install.packages(lib, dependencies=TRUE)
sapply(load.lib, require, character=TRUE)
## tidyverse lubridate   suncalc        gt patchwork     rstan      here 
##      TRUE      TRUE      TRUE      TRUE      TRUE      TRUE      TRUE
rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
purrr::walk(list.files(here("03_R_functions"), full.names = TRUE), source)

# Create dated output directory
run_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")
output_dir <- here("05_output", run_date, "gear-type-CPUE-model")
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)


# --- Timing ---
timer_log <- list()
timer_start <- function(label) { timer_log[[label]] <<- list(start = Sys.time()); invisible() }
timer_stop <- function(label) {
  timer_log[[label]]$end <<- Sys.time()
  elapsed <- difftime(timer_log[[label]]$end, timer_log[[label]]$start, units = "mins")
  timer_log[[label]]$elapsed <<- elapsed
  cat(sprintf("  [TIMER] %s: %.1f min\n", label, as.numeric(elapsed)))
  invisible()
}
run_start_time <- Sys.time()

# ===========================================================================
# 0.1 PARAMETERS — All user-configurable settings in one place
# ===========================================================================

params <- list(
  # --- Metadata ---
  project_name      = "Coastal Recreational Crab",
  fishery_name      = "Rec Crab Grays Harbor Westport 2024-25",
  est_date_start    = "2024-09-16",
  est_date_end      = "2025-09-15",
  season_filter     = "2024-25",

  # --- Gear restriction dates ---
  # Pots prohibited Sep 16 – Nov 30; allowed Dec 1 onward
  pot_open_date     = "2024-12-01",

  # --- Commercial/charter season ---
  commercial_opener = "2025-01-1",
  census_start_date = "2024-12-01",
  census_end_date   = "2025-02-08",

  # --- Catch groups ---
  estimate_red_rock = FALSE,

  # --- Day type classification ---
  days_wkend        = c("Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"),
  min_fishing_time  = 0.5,   # minimum crabber-hours to include an interview

  # --- Time stratification ---
  period_pe         = "week",    # stat week for PE temporal resolution
  period_bss        = "month",   # default BSS period (overridden per sub-season)
  sections          = c(1),

  # --- BSS model settings ---
  bss_model_file    = "crab_bss_gear_resolved.stan",
  bss_chains        = 4,
  bss_cores         = 4,
  bss_max_interviews = NULL,  # v5.1: use full dataset (was 1000)
  bss_max_count_seq  = 3,     # cap count sequences per day

  # --- BSS convergence tuning ---
  bss_iter_default          = 2000,
  bss_warmup_default        = 1000,
  bss_max_treedepth_default = 10,
  bss_adapt_delta_default   = 0.9,

  # Per-fit overrides. The shore all-gear fit has 4 gear types across 10
  # monthly periods with 1,600+ interviews — the most complex posterior
  # geometry. It needs more iterations, higher adapt_delta, and a deeper
  # treedepth to avoid trajectory truncation (93.9% treedepth saturation
  # at max_treedepth=10).
  # Ring-net sub-season fits are data-sparse and benefit from tighter
  # adaptation but converge faster due to fewer parameters.
  bss_iter_shore_allgear    = 4000,
  bss_warmup_shore_allgear  = 2000,
  bss_treedepth_shore_allgear = 14,
  bss_delta_shore_allgear     = 0.95,

  bss_iter_ringnet          = 4000,
  bss_warmup_ringnet        = 2000,
  bss_treedepth_ringnet     = 12,
  bss_delta_ringnet         = 0.95,

  # --- Minimum interview threshold for BSS ---
  bss_min_interviews = 20,

  # --- Minimum effective-N per gear type for independent modeling ---
  # Gear types with fewer than this many effective interviews (sum of
  # fractional weights) are collapsed. Raised from 3 to 15 because
  # fractional weights from multi-gear interviews can push phantom
  # gear types above a low threshold. With 15, a gear type needs
  # either ~15 single-gear interviews or ~30 half-weighted multi-gear
  # interviews to qualify.
  bss_min_gear_effective_n = 15,

  # --- R_G fixed value for boat fits (Issue 5) ---
  R_G_fixed_boat = 1.3,

  # --- v5.2: Incomplete trip filter ---
  # Incomplete trips have systematically lower CPUE (-20% for pots/traps)
  # because soak-time-dependent gear hasn't finished.
  # TRUE = exclude incomplete trips from CPUE estimation (recommended).
  # FALSE = include all trips (v5.1 behavior).
  filter_incomplete_trips = TRUE,

  # --- B1.9 parity: weekend/holiday effect on CPUE (pooled has this; v5.4 did not) ---
  # TRUE  = lambda_C = exp(mu_C + omega_C + B1_C * weekend), matching the pooled model.
  # FALSE = lambda_C = exp(mu_C + omega_C), the v5.4 behavior (CPUE constrained
  #         identical on weekends and weekdays).
  # B1_C is sampled either way; when FALSE it drops out of the likelihood, so
  # log_lik_catch is the reduced model's and elpd_loo from a TRUE/FALSE pair of
  # runs is directly comparable. Adjudicate with loo_summary_*.csv, or read the
  # B1_C posterior directly (does its 95% CI exclude zero?).
  estimate_B1_C = TRUE,

  # --- Ingress/egress shore day length (shared: 03_R_functions/bss_day_length.R) ---
  # Shore effort is expanded by an effective day length derived from I/E surveys
  # (~3.5-5.0 h), not civil twilight (9-17 h), which overestimates it by ~2x.
  # The fallback ladder is automatic: regression (n >= ie_min_obs_for_regression)
  # -> grand mean (3 <= n < threshold) -> civil twilight (n < 3, with a warning).
  # These values are identical to the pooled driver's so the two agree exactly.
  ie_data_file      = "ingress_egress.xlsx",
  ie_sheet          = "data",
  ie_shore_location = "WDF20",
  ie_boat_location  = "WBL",
  use_ie_day_length = TRUE,
  ie_min_obs_for_regression = 5,

  # Civil-twilight clamp; only binds on the fallback rung.
  day_length_min_hours = 9.0,
  day_length_max_hours = 17.0,

  # Project centroid (Westport), used for the civil-twilight computation.
  centroid_lat = 46.904,
  centroid_lon = -124.105,
  local_tz     = "America/Los_Angeles",

  # --- v5.2: Convergence divergence criterion ---
  # Maximum number of divergent transitions allowed for BSS to pass.
  # 0 = strict (any divergence fails), 5 = permissive (a handful of
  # divergences in 4000+ post-warmup draws is tolerable when R-hat and
  # ESS are clean). Set higher for exploratory runs, lower for production.
  max_divergences = 5,

  # --- B1.8 scale-aware convergence gate (ported from pooled) ---
  # These SUPERSEDE the count-based max_divergences for the PE/BSS decision. A
  # fit passes only if R-hat/n_eff pass AND the divergence fraction is below
  # max_divergence_fraction AND the divergences do not move the reported totals
  # by more than max_divergence_total_impact_sd (in posterior-SD units).
  max_divergence_total_impact_sd = 0.10,
  max_divergence_fraction        = 0.05,

  # --- AR temporal resolution (shared selector: 03_R_functions/bss_ar_resolution.R) ---
  # ar_adaptive = FALSE (default) preserves v5.x behavior EXACTLY: each sub-season
  # uses its declared period_bss (biweekly for ring-net, monthly for all-gear).
  # Setting TRUE hands the choice to the data-driven selector, subject to
  # ar_max_resolution. That is INFERENCE-CHANGING (it changes the latent AR
  # dimension P_n, hence the posterior), so it is opt-in and should be validated
  # against a fixed-period run before adoption.
  ar_adaptive = FALSE,

  # Caps used ONLY when ar_adaptive = TRUE. Conservative for this model because
  # the gear-resolved latent AR is P_n x (G*S): with 4 gear types a daily AR is
  # ~4x the pooled latent dimension at the same P_n.
  ar_max_resolution      = list(shore = "weekly", private_boat = "monthly"),
  ar_daily_min_coverage  = 0.25,   # fraction of days with effort obs
  ar_daily_min_obs       = 20,     # absolute minimum for daily
  ar_weekly_min_per_week = 1.5,    # average obs per week for weekly

  # Experiment toggle: force a population's AR resolution, bypassing both the
  # fixed period_bss and the cap. NULL (production) is a no-op.
  ar_force = NULL
)

# --- Run-level overrides from run_estimation.R (single source of truth) -------
# When launched via the orchestrator, `run_config` exists in this render
# environment and overrides ONLY the run-level keys it defines (season window,
# seed, chains/cores, ...). All model-specific tuning above is left untouched.
# Knit this .Rmd standalone (no run_config) and the in-file defaults are used.
if (exists("run_config")) params <- modifyList(params, run_config)

# Crabbing holidays for 2024-25 season (high-effort days)
crabbing_holiday_dates <- as.Date(c(
  "2024-11-29",  # Native American Heritage Day
  "2024-12-31",  # New Year's Eve
  "2025-01-01",  # New Year's Day
  "2025-02-08",  # Super Bowl Eve
  "2025-05-24",  # Memorial Day weekend - Saturday
  "2025-05-25",  # Memorial Day weekend - Sunday
  "2025-05-26",  # Memorial Day
  "2025-06-15",  # Father's Day
  "2025-07-04",  # Independence Day (Friday = weekend, counts as holiday)
  "2025-09-01"   # Labor Day
))

# Run-level override: when launched via run_estimation.R, use the config's
# holiday list so pooled / gear-resolved / weather never drift. Standalone knit
# keeps the in-file list above.
if (exists("run_config") && !is.null(run_config$crabbing_holiday_dates)) {
  crabbing_holiday_dates <- run_config$crabbing_holiday_dates
}

# --- Derived: catch groups vector used throughout ---
catch_groups <- if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
  c("Dungeness_Kept", "Red_Rock_Kept")
} else {
  "Dungeness_Kept"
}

# --- Derived: sub-season date ranges ---
# v5.1: Each sub-season specifies its own BSS period type.
# Ring-net sub-season uses biweekly periods (fewer months, sparse data).
# All-gear sub-season uses monthly periods (longer, more data).
subseason_1 <- list(
  name = "ring_net_only",
  start = as.Date(params$est_date_start),
  end   = as.Date(params$pot_open_date) - 1,
  period_bss = "biweekly",
  # Gear types prohibited by regulation during this sub-season.
  # Pots are illegal Sep 16 – Nov 30. Any "Pot" mentions in interviews
  # during this period are recording errors or crabbers naming gear they
  # own rather than what they deployed. Forcing the model to fit a Pot
  # CPUE process with <6 fractional interviews causes catastrophic
  # convergence failure (divergences, overflow in C_sum_gear).
  gear_exclude = c("Pot")
)
subseason_2 <- list(
  name = "all_gear",
  start = as.Date(params$pot_open_date),
  end   = as.Date(params$est_date_end),
  period_bss = "month",
  gear_exclude = character(0)  # All gear types legal
)

cat(sprintf("Sub-season 1 (ring-net): %s to %s (%d days, periods=%s)\n",
            subseason_1$start, subseason_1$end,
            as.integer(subseason_1$end - subseason_1$start + 1),
            subseason_1$period_bss))
## Sub-season 1 (ring-net): 2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30 (76 days, periods=biweekly)
cat(sprintf("Sub-season 2 (all-gear): %s to %s (%d days, periods=%s)\n",
            subseason_2$start, subseason_2$end,
            as.integer(subseason_2$end - subseason_2$start + 1),
            subseason_2$period_bss))
## Sub-season 2 (all-gear): 2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15 (289 days, periods=month)
cat(sprintf("Catch groups: %s\n", paste(catch_groups, collapse = ", ")))
## Catch groups: Dungeness_Kept
# ===========================================================================
# 1. DATA INGESTION — Read CSVs and classify interviews by population
#
# Populations:
#   shore        = Dock + Jetty + Beach crabbers
#   private_boat = Private boats (launched from BL, interviewed at dock or BL)
#   comm_charter = Commercial crab vessels + charter boats (Marina)
# ===========================================================================


fetch_crab_data_v2 <- function(params) {

  cat("Reading data...\n")

  # --- Read effort CSV (dates in YYYY-MM-DD from re-exported file) ---
  effort_raw <- read_csv(here("04_input_files","effort_combined.csv"), show_col_types=FALSE) |>
    filter(season == params$season_filter) |> mutate(date = as.Date(date))

  # --- Read interview CSV (dates in M/D/YYYY from iForm export) ---
  interview_raw <- read_csv(here("04_input_files","interview_combined.csv"), show_col_types=FALSE,
    col_types = cols(date=col_date(format="%m/%d/%Y"), crabbers=col_double(),
      number_of_gear=col_double(), dungeness_kept=col_double(), red_rock_kept=col_double(),
      hours_fished=col_double(), crabber_hours=col_double(), gear_hours=col_double(),
      completed_trip=col_character(), total_vehicles=col_double(), crabbing_holiday=col_integer())) |>
    filter(season == params$season_filter)

  # --- Filter to Grays Harbor sites ---
  gh_effort <- effort_raw |> filter(creel_area %in% c(
    "Westport Docks Float 20","Westport Docks Float 17-21",
    "Westport Boat Launch","Westport Marina","Westport Jetty",
    "Ocean Shores Boat Launch","Damon Point"))

  gh_interview <- interview_raw |> filter(creel_location == "Grays Harbor")

  # --- Classify each interview into a population ---
  gh_interview <- gh_interview |>
    mutate(
      event_date = as.Date(as.character(date), format = "%Y-%m-%d"),
      boat_type_clean = case_when(
        str_detect(boat_type, "(?i)commer") ~ "Commercial",
        str_detect(boat_type, "(?i)charter") ~ "Charter",
        str_detect(boat_type, "(?i)guide") ~ "Charter",
        str_detect(boat_type, "(?i)private") ~ "Private",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      population = case_when(
        boat_type_clean %in% c("Commercial","Charter") ~ "comm_charter",
        crabbing_mode == "Boat" & (is.na(boat_type_clean) | boat_type_clean == "Private") ~ "private_boat",
        crabbing_mode %in% c("Dock","Jetty","Beach") ~ "shore",
        TRUE ~ "shore"
      )
    )

  cat("  Grays Harbor interviews by population:\n")
  gh_interview |> count(population) |> mutate(l=sprintf("    %s: %d",population,n)) |>
    pull(l) |> walk(cat,"\n")

  # --- Process interviews: compute fishing time, catch, trip status ---
  gh_interview <- gh_interview |>
    filter(!is.na(crabbers), as.numeric(crabbers) > 0) |>
    mutate(
      interview_id = paste0(survey_id,"_",interview_num),
      section_num = 1,
      angler_count = as.integer(crabbers),
      number_of_gear = as.numeric(number_of_gear),
      hours_fished = as.numeric(hours_fished),
      crabber_hours_calc = as.numeric(crabber_hours),
      fishing_time_total = case_when(
        !is.na(crabber_hours_calc) & crabber_hours_calc > 0 ~ crabber_hours_calc,
        !is.na(hours_fished) & !is.na(angler_count) ~ hours_fished * angler_count,
        TRUE ~ NA_real_
      ),
      gear_time_total = case_when(
        !is.na(as.numeric(gear_hours)) & as.numeric(gear_hours) > 0 ~ as.numeric(gear_hours),
        !is.na(hours_fished) & !is.na(number_of_gear) ~ hours_fished * number_of_gear,
        TRUE ~ NA_real_
      ),
      dungeness_kept = replace_na(as.numeric(dungeness_kept), 0),
      red_rock_kept = replace_na(as.numeric(red_rock_kept), 0),
      trip_status = case_when(
        completed_trip=="1"~"Complete", completed_trip=="0"~"Incomplete", TRUE~NA_character_
      ),
      angler_final_int = case_when(population=="shore"~1L, population=="private_boat"~2L, TRUE~NA_integer_)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(fishing_time_total), fishing_time_total >= params$min_fishing_time)

  # --- SHORE EFFORT: Pair Float 20 + Float 17-21 gear counts ---
  dock_effort <- gh_effort |>
    filter(creel_area %in% c("Westport Docks Float 20","Westport Docks Float 17-21")) |>
    mutate(event_date = date,
           count_time_posix = as.POSIXct(paste(date,count_time), format="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz="America/Los_Angeles")) |>
    filter(!is.na(count_time_posix))

  f20 <- dock_effort |> filter(creel_area=="Westport Docks Float 20") |>
    arrange(event_date,count_time_posix) |> group_by(event_date) |>
    mutate(count_sequence=row_number()) |> ungroup()

  f17 <- dock_effort |> filter(creel_area=="Westport Docks Float 17-21")

  if(nrow(f17) > 0) {
    f17_paired <- f17 |>
      left_join(f20 |> select(event_date,count_sequence,f20_time=count_time_posix),
                by="event_date", relationship="many-to-many") |>
      mutate(time_diff=abs(as.numeric(difftime(count_time_posix,f20_time,units="mins")))) |>
      group_by(event_date,survey_id,count_time) |> slice_min(time_diff,n=1,with_ties=FALSE) |> ungroup() |>
      select(event_date,count_sequence,f17_gear=total_gear_count)
  } else {
    f17_paired <- tibble(event_date=Date(),count_sequence=integer(),f17_gear=numeric())
  }

  shore_effort <- f20 |> select(event_date,count_sequence,f20_gear=total_gear_count) |>
    left_join(f17_paired, by=c("event_date","count_sequence")) |>
    mutate(f17_gear=replace_na(f17_gear,0), count_quantity=f20_gear+f17_gear,
           section_num=1, count_type="Gear Count", population="shore")

  # --- BOAT EFFORT: Trailer counts at boat launches ---
  boat_effort <- gh_effort |>
    filter(creel_area %in% c("Westport Boat Launch","Ocean Shores Boat Launch")) |>
    mutate(event_date = date,
           count_time_posix = as.POSIXct(paste(date,count_time), format="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz="America/Los_Angeles"),
           count_quantity = as.numeric(boat_trailer_count)) |>
    filter(!is.na(count_time_posix), !is.na(count_quantity), count_quantity >= 0) |>
    arrange(event_date,count_time_posix) |> group_by(event_date) |>
    mutate(count_sequence=row_number()) |> ungroup() |>
    select(event_date,count_sequence,count_quantity) |>
    mutate(section_num=1, count_type="Trailer Count", population="private_boat")

  # --- COMMERCIAL TALLY ---
  comm_tally <- read_csv(here("04_input_files","wes_commercial_tally.csv"), show_col_types=FALSE) |>
    mutate(date = as.Date(date))

  cat(sprintf("\n  Shore effort obs: %d (%d days)\n", nrow(shore_effort), n_distinct(shore_effort$event_date)))
  cat(sprintf("  Boat effort obs: %d (%d days)\n", nrow(boat_effort), n_distinct(boat_effort$event_date)))
  cat(sprintf("  Commercial tally days: %d\n", nrow(comm_tally)))

  # --- CATCH tables ---
  catch <- bind_rows(
    gh_interview |> filter(dungeness_kept>0) |>
      transmute(interview_id,event_date,population,species="Dungeness",fate="Kept",
                fish_count=as.integer(dungeness_kept),catch_group="Dungeness_Kept"),
    if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
      gh_interview |> filter(red_rock_kept>0) |>
        transmute(interview_id,event_date,population,species="Red_Rock",fate="Kept",
                  fish_count=as.integer(red_rock_kept),catch_group="Red_Rock_Kept")
    } else { tibble() }
  )

  return(list(
    shore_effort = shore_effort,
    boat_effort = boat_effort,
    interview = gh_interview,
    catch = catch,
    comm_tally = comm_tally,
    ll = tibble(centroid_lat=46.904, centroid_lon=-124.105)
  ))
}


# ===========================================================================
# 2. PREP DAYS — Build calendar for a given date range
#
# v5.1: Added day_type_idx (integer: 1=weekday, 2=weekend, 3=holiday)
#        Day length cap raised to 17h (was 16h) for summer at 46.9°N
# ===========================================================================

prep_days_crab <- function(date_begin, date_end, weekends, holiday_dates, period_pe, sections,
                           L_eff_model = NULL) {
  date_begin <- as.Date(date_begin); date_end <- as.Date(date_end)
  days <- tibble(
    event_date = seq.Date(date_begin, date_end, by="day"),
    day = weekdays(event_date),
    day_type = case_when(
      event_date %in% holiday_dates ~ "holiday",
      day %in% weekends ~ "weekend", TRUE ~ "weekday"),
    # v5.1: Integer day type index for Stan (1=weekday, 2=weekend, 3=holiday)
    day_type_idx = case_when(
      day_type == "weekday" ~ 1L,
      day_type == "weekend" ~ 2L,
      day_type == "holiday" ~ 3L),
    day_type_num_weekend = as.integer(day_type %in% c("weekend","holiday")),
    day_type_num_holiday = as.integer(day_type == "holiday"),
    week = as.numeric(format(event_date,"%W")),
    month = as.numeric(format(event_date,"%m")),
    year = as.numeric(format(event_date,"%Y")),
    period = case_when(
      period_pe == "month" ~ as.numeric(format(event_date, "%m")),
      period_pe == "week"  ~ as.numeric(format(event_date, "%W")),
      TRUE ~ as.numeric(format(event_date, "%W"))
    ),
    day_index = as.integer(seq_along(event_date)),
    week_index = as.integer(factor(
      paste(year, sprintf("%02d", week)),
      levels = unique(paste(year, sprintf("%02d", week)))
    )),
    month_index = as.integer(factor(paste(year,sprintf("%02d",month)),
                  levels=unique(paste(year,sprintf("%02d",month))))),
    day_length = NA_real_
  )
  # Civil twilight + L_effective assignment. Shared with the pooled driver:
  # 03_R_functions/bss_day_length.R. Sets day_length, day_length_civil_twilight,
  # L_mu and L_prior_sigma. Falls back to civil twilight (clamped to
  # [day_length_min_hours, day_length_max_hours], default [9, 17]) only when
  # L_eff_model is NULL, i.e. no usable I/E data.
  days <- bss_assign_day_length(days, L_eff_model, params)

  for(s in sections) days[[paste0("open_section_",s)]] <- TRUE
  days
}


# ===========================================================================
# 3. SUMMARIZE DATA — Filter to a given population × sub-season
# ===========================================================================

prep_population_summary <- function(dwg, population_name, date_start, date_end, params) {
  ds <- as.Date(date_start); de <- as.Date(date_end)
  summ <- list()

  if(population_name == "shore") {
    summ$effort_index <- dwg$shore_effort |> filter(between(event_date, ds, de))
  } else if(population_name == "private_boat") {
    summ$effort_index <- dwg$boat_effort |> filter(between(event_date, ds, de))
  }

  int_pop <- dwg$interview |>
    filter(population == population_name, between(event_date, ds, de))

  catch_wide <- dwg$catch |>
    filter(population == population_name) |>
    group_by(interview_id, catch_group) |>
    summarise(fish_count=sum(fish_count), .groups="drop") |>
    pivot_wider(names_from=catch_group, values_from=fish_count, values_fill=0)

  summ$interview <- int_pop |>
    left_join(catch_wide, by="interview_id") |>
    mutate(across(any_of(catch_groups), ~replace_na(.,0)))

  ratio_data <- summ$interview |> filter(!is.na(number_of_gear), number_of_gear>0, angler_count>0)
  summ$crabbers_per_gear <- if(nrow(ratio_data)>0) sum(ratio_data$angler_count)/sum(ratio_data$number_of_gear) else 1.0

  cat(sprintf("\n  %s [%s to %s]: %d effort obs, %d interviews, crab/gear=%.2f\n",
              population_name, ds, de, nrow(summ$effort_index), nrow(summ$interview), summ$crabbers_per_gear))

  return(summ)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 4. PE METHOD — Point estimate using stratified expansion
# ===========================================================================

run_pe <- function(summ, days, params, population_name) {
  results <- list()

  # v5.2 Fix 1: Filter incomplete trips for CPUE estimation (PE method)
  interview_for_cpue <- summ$interview
  if(isTRUE(params$filter_incomplete_trips)) {
    interview_for_cpue <- interview_for_cpue |>
      filter(trip_status == "Complete" | is.na(trip_status))
  }

  # Day-length basis for the PE effort expansion.
  # bss_assign_day_length() overwrites days$day_length with the SHORE-derived
  # L_effective (~4 h) whenever an I/E model exists. That is correct for shore
  # and wrong for boats, whose gear soaks continuously. run_pe() is
  # population-agnostic, so select the basis explicitly here: shore gets the
  # effective day length, boats keep civil twilight (their prior behavior),
  # which isolates the 5a change to the shore component.
  #
  # KNOWN ISSUE (not fixed here): the boat PE below still expands with
  # crabber-hours (est_crabbers * L) via summ$crabbers_per_gear. The pooled
  # driver moved the boat PE to gear-hours (mean_count * gear_per_group * 24)
  # and replaced crabbers_per_gear with crabbers_per_group. Porting that is a
  # separate change with its own validation; until then the boat PE is expected
  # to disagree with the boat BSS.
  is_shore_pe <- (population_name == "shore")
  days <- days |>
    mutate(L_pe = if(is_shore_pe) day_length else day_length_civil_twilight)

  daily_effort <- summ$effort_index |>
    filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
    group_by(event_date, section_num) |>
    summarise(mean_count=mean(count_quantity), n_counts=n(), .groups="drop") |>
    mutate(est_crabbers = mean_count * summ$crabbers_per_gear) |>
    left_join(days |> select(event_date,day_type,L_pe,period), by="event_date") |>
    mutate(est_daily_crabber_hours = est_crabbers * L_pe,
           est_daily_gear_hours = mean_count * L_pe)

  total_days_strat <- days |> filter(open_section_1) |>
    group_by(period, day_type) |> summarise(n_total_days=n(), .groups="drop")

  effort_strat <- daily_effort |>
    group_by(section_num, period, day_type) |>
    summarise(mean_daily=mean(est_daily_crabber_hours,na.rm=TRUE),
              sd_daily=sd(est_daily_crabber_hours,na.rm=TRUE),
              n_sampled=n(), .groups="drop") |>
    left_join(total_days_strat, by=c("period","day_type")) |>
    mutate(est_total = mean_daily * n_total_days,
           se_total = sqrt((n_total_days^2)*replace_na(sd_daily^2,0)/pmax(n_sampled,1)))

  results$effort_total <- sum(effort_strat$est_total, na.rm=TRUE)
  results$effort_se <- sqrt(sum(effort_strat$se_total^2, na.rm=TRUE))

  for(cg in catch_groups) {
    if(!cg %in% names(summ$interview)) { results[[cg]] <- 0; next }

    daily_cpue <- interview_for_cpue |>
      group_by(event_date, section_num) |>
      summarise(catch=sum(.data[[cg]],na.rm=TRUE), hrs=sum(fishing_time_total,na.rm=TRUE),
                n_int=n(), .groups="drop") |>
      mutate(cpue=if_else(hrs>0, catch/hrs, 0)) |>
      left_join(days |> select(event_date,day_type,period), by="event_date")

    cpue_strat <- daily_cpue |>
      group_by(section_num, period, day_type) |>
      summarise(mean_cpue=weighted.mean(cpue,w=n_int,na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop")

    catch_strat <- effort_strat |>
      left_join(cpue_strat, by=c("section_num","period","day_type")) |>
      mutate(est_catch = est_total * replace_na(mean_cpue, 0))

    results[[cg]] <- sum(catch_strat$est_catch, na.rm=TRUE)
  }

  rr_str <- if(params$estimate_red_rock) sprintf(", RR=%s", format(round(results$Red_Rock_Kept),big.mark=",")) else ""
  cat(sprintf("  PE %s: Effort=%s hrs, Dung=%s%s\n", population_name,
              format(round(results$effort_total),big.mark=","),
              format(round(results$Dungeness_Kept),big.mark=","), rr_str))

  return(results)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 5. BSS PREP — Build the Stan data list for crab_bss_gear_resolved.stan
#
# v5.2 CHANGES (from v5.1):
#   - Incomplete trip filter (filter_incomplete_trips parameter)
#   - n_weighted_gear array for Dirichlet pi_gear uncertainty in Stan
# v5.1 CHANGES:
#   - Weighted gear assignment: gear_weights matrix replaces gear_type_IntC
#   - Empirical pi_gear_data[P_n, 3, G_gear] with Laplace smoothing
#   - Per-sub-season period type (biweekly for ring-net, monthly for all-gear)
#   - estimate_R_G flag and R_G_fixed for boat fits
#   - Cauchy prior scales tightened to 2 (was 5)
#   - Word-boundary regex for robust gear-type detection
#   - bss_max_interviews removed (full dataset used)
# ===========================================================================

prep_bss_crab <- function(days, summ, est_catch_group, params, population_name, period_type,
                          gear_exclude = character(0), ie_data = NULL) {

  eff <- summ$effort_index |> filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq)
  D <- nrow(days); G <- 1L; S <- 1L

  # --- BSS period / AR temporal resolution ---------------------------------
  # Shared selector: 03_R_functions/bss_ar_resolution.R. eff_d is built first
  # because the selector reports effort coverage. Default (params$ar_adaptive =
  # FALSE) passes the sub-season's explicit period_bss, reproducing the v5.x
  # fixed-period behavior exactly. Setting ar_adaptive = TRUE lets the data
  # choose, subject to params$ar_max_resolution. params$ar_force overrides both.
  eff_d <- eff |> left_join(days |> select(event_date,day_index), by="event_date") |> filter(!is.na(day_index))

  ar_sel <- bss_select_ar_resolution(
    days             = days,
    eff_d            = eff_d,
    population_name  = population_name,
    params           = params,
    fixed_resolution = if (isTRUE(params$ar_adaptive)) NULL else period_type
  )
  ar_resolution <- ar_sel$resolution
  P_n  <- ar_sel$P_n
  pvec <- ar_sel$pvec

  # v5.3: is_shore is computed early so that the boat-specific
  # gear_time_total filter and the L/h branches below can use it.
  is_shore <- (population_name == "shore")

  int_cg <- summ$interview |>
    mutate(fish_count = .data[[est_catch_group]]) |>
    filter(!is.na(fishing_time_total), fishing_time_total > 0)

  int_d <- int_cg |>
    left_join(days |> select(event_date,day_index,day_type,day_type_idx), by="event_date") |>
    filter(!is.na(day_index)) |> distinct(interview_id, .keep_all=TRUE)

  # =========================================================================
  # v5.2 FIX 1: Incomplete trip filter
  # Crabbers still fishing at interview time have systematically lower CPUE
  # (-20% for pots/traps) because soak-time-dependent gear hasn't finished.
  # Ring nets are less affected (+4% bias) since they are checked frequently.
  # Including incomplete trips biases harvest estimates downward by ~7%.
  # =========================================================================
  n_before_trip_filter <- nrow(int_d)
  trip_status_counts <- int_d |> count(trip_status, name = "n")
  n_incomplete <- sum(int_d$trip_status == "Incomplete", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_complete <- sum(int_d$trip_status == "Complete", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_missing_status <- sum(is.na(int_d$trip_status))

  cat(sprintf("  Trip completion: %d complete, %d incomplete (%.0f%%), %d missing status\n",
              n_complete, n_incomplete,
              n_incomplete / max(n_complete + n_incomplete, 1) * 100,
              n_missing_status))

  # Report CPUE difference if both groups have data
  cpue_by_status <- int_d |>
    filter(fishing_time_total >= 0.5, fish_count >= 0) |>
    mutate(cpue = fish_count / fishing_time_total) |>
    group_by(trip_status) |>
    summarise(mean_cpue = mean(cpue), n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  if(nrow(cpue_by_status) >= 2) {
    cpue_comp <- cpue_by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Complete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
    cpue_inc <- cpue_by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Incomplete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
    if(length(cpue_comp) > 0 && length(cpue_inc) > 0 && cpue_comp > 0) {
      bias_pct <- (cpue_inc - cpue_comp) / cpue_comp * 100
      cat(sprintf("  CPUE: complete=%.3f, incomplete=%.3f (bias=%+.1f%%)\n",
                  cpue_comp, cpue_inc, bias_pct))
    }
  }

  if(isTRUE(params$filter_incomplete_trips)) {
    # Keep complete trips + missing status (which may be complete)
    int_d <- int_d |> filter(trip_status == "Complete" | is.na(trip_status))
    cat(sprintf("  Incomplete trip filter ON: %d → %d interviews (removed %d)\n",
                n_before_trip_filter, nrow(int_d), n_before_trip_filter - nrow(int_d)))
  } else {
    cat("  Incomplete trip filter OFF (v5.1 behavior)\n")
  }

  # --- 5b: I/E observations for this population and sub-season ---------------
  # Matched to day_index within the sub-season window. Shore only: the boat has no
  # shore I/E stream, so IE_n = 0 and sigma_IE is identified by its prior alone
  # (which is why that prior must be unconditional; see the Stan model's B1.6 note).
  ie_match <- tibble(day_index = integer(), ie_crabber_hours = numeric())
  # I/E stream is SHORE ONLY. The boat's ie_crabber_hours is boat-hours
  # (sum(boat_flow * 0.25)), but the likelihood predicts lambda_E * L, and for
  # boats lambda_E is GEAR in the water with L = 24, i.e. gear-hours. The two
  # differ by R_G_boat (~4 gear per group), so feeding boat I/E in would pull
  # boat effort down ~4x, and with estimate_L = 0 there is no L to absorb it.
  # This is behavior-neutral for the 2024-25 window (the only positive WBL days
  # are 2023-12-20 and 2025-12-19, both outside it) but NOT for 2025-26, which
  # contains 2025-12-19. Boat I/E can be used only once the model predicts
  # boat-hours (lambda_E * L / R_G_boat) rather than gear-hours.
  if(!is.null(ie_data) && nrow(ie_data) > 0 && is_shore) {
    ie_match <- ie_data |>
      filter(population == "shore") |>
      inner_join(days |> select(event_date, day_index), by = "event_date") |>
      filter(!is.na(day_index), ie_crabber_hours > 0)
  }
  IE_n <- nrow(ie_match)
  cat(sprintf("  I/E observations: %d%s\n", IE_n,
              if(IE_n > 0) sprintf(" days (crabber-hrs %.0f-%.0f)",
                                   min(ie_match$ie_crabber_hours),
                                   max(ie_match$ie_crabber_hours)) else ""))

  intA <- int_d |> filter(!is.na(number_of_gear), number_of_gear>0, angler_count>0)

  # v5.3: For boat fits, the CPUE denominator is gear-hours, not crabber-hours.
  # gear_time_total is computed in load_creel_data() from gear_hours (preferred)
  # or hours_fished * number_of_gear (fallback). Drop boat interviews lacking it
  # so the IntC likelihood (c ~ NegBin(lambda_C * h, r_C)) is well-defined.
  if(!is_shore) {
    n_before_gh <- nrow(int_d)
    int_d <- int_d |> filter(!is.na(gear_time_total), gear_time_total > 0)
    cat(sprintf("  Boat gear-hours filter: %d → %d interviews (require non-NA gear_time_total)\n",
                n_before_gh, nrow(int_d)))
    intA <- intA |> filter(interview_id %in% int_d$interview_id)
  }

  # v5.1: bss_max_interviews = NULL means use full dataset (Issue 8)
  if(!is.null(params$bss_max_interviews) && nrow(int_d)>params$bss_max_interviews) {
    set.seed(42)
    int_d <- int_d |> slice_sample(n=params$bss_max_interviews)
    intA <- intA |> filter(interview_id %in% int_d$interview_id)
  }

  # v5.3: is_shore now defined earlier in the function (see comment above).

  # =========================================================================
  # v5.1 GEAR-TYPE CLASSIFICATION: Word-boundary regex + weighted assignment
  # =========================================================================
  # Detect each gear type with word-boundary patterns to avoid false matches
  # (e.g., "hotpot" would not match \bpot\b). The iForm uses controlled

  # vocabulary, but word boundaries make this robust to future data changes.

  int_d <- int_d |>
    mutate(
      has_pot      = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bpot\\b") &
                                !str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bslip\\s*ring\\b")),
      has_ring_net = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bring\\s*net\\b")),
      has_trap     = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\b(trap|star)\\b")),
      has_snare    = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bsnare\\b")),
      # Number of gear types reported per interview
      n_gear_types_reported = has_pot + has_ring_net + has_trap + has_snare,
      n_gear_types_reported = pmax(n_gear_types_reported, 1L)  # fallback for unrecognized
    )

  # Compute effective sample sizes (sum of fractional weights per gear type)
  gear_effective_n <- c(
    Pot       = sum(int_d$has_pot / int_d$n_gear_types_reported),
    `Ring Net` = sum(int_d$has_ring_net / int_d$n_gear_types_reported),
    Trap      = sum(int_d$has_trap / int_d$n_gear_types_reported),
    Snare     = sum(int_d$has_snare / int_d$n_gear_types_reported)
  )

  # =========================================================================
  # REGULATORY EXCLUSION: Remove gear types prohibited in this sub-season.
  # During the ring-net sub-season (Sep 16 – Nov 30), pots are illegal.
  # Any "Pot" detections are recording artifacts from multi-gear crabbers
  # naming gear they own rather than gear actually deployed. Attempting to
  # fit a Pot CPUE AR(1) process with <6 fractional interviews causes
  # catastrophic sampler failure: 800+ divergent transitions, overflow in
  # C_sum_gear, and contamination of other gear-type parameters.
  # =========================================================================
  if(length(gear_exclude) > 0) {
    excluded <- intersect(names(gear_effective_n), gear_exclude)
    if(length(excluded) > 0) {
      gear_effective_n[excluded] <- 0
      # Zero out the has_ flags for excluded types so their fractional
      # weight redistributes to remaining gear types in the weight matrix.
      # E.g., "Pot, Ring Net" becomes "Ring Net" (weight 1.0 instead of 0.5).
      has_columns_excl <- c(Pot="has_pot", `Ring Net`="has_ring_net",
                            Trap="has_trap", Snare="has_snare")
      for(gt in excluded) {
        if(gt %in% names(has_columns_excl)) {
          int_d[[has_columns_excl[gt]]] <- 0L
        }
      }
      # Recompute n_gear_types_reported after exclusion
      int_d <- int_d |>
        mutate(n_gear_types_reported = pmax(has_pot + has_ring_net + has_trap + has_snare, 1L))
      cat(sprintf("  Regulatory exclusion: %s removed (prohibited this sub-season)\n",
                  paste(excluded, collapse = ", ")))
    }
  }

  # Gear types with sufficient effective interviews are modeled independently.
  # Threshold is set conservatively because fractional weights from multi-gear
  # interviews can inflate effective N beyond what the data truly supports.
  min_gear_n <- params$bss_min_gear_effective_n %||% 15
  gear_types_present <- names(gear_effective_n[gear_effective_n >= min_gear_n])

  # If fewer than 2 gear types qualify, collapse to a single "All" category
  if(length(gear_types_present) < 2) {
    gear_type_labels <- "All"
    G_gear <- 1L
    gear_weight_matrix <- matrix(1.0, nrow = nrow(int_d), ncol = 1)
    colnames(gear_weight_matrix) <- "All"
    cat("  Insufficient gear type diversity — using single 'All' category\n")
  } else {
    gear_type_labels <- sort(gear_types_present)
    G_gear <- length(gear_type_labels)

    # Build weight matrix: each interview gets equal weight across its
    # reported gear types (e.g., "Pot, Ring Net" → 0.5 Pot + 0.5 Ring Net)
    has_columns <- c(Pot="has_pot", `Ring Net`="has_ring_net",
                     Trap="has_trap", Snare="has_snare")
    weight_cols <- list()
    for(gt in gear_type_labels) {
      weight_cols[[gt]] <- int_d[[has_columns[gt]]] / int_d$n_gear_types_reported
    }
    gear_weight_matrix <- do.call(cbind, weight_cols)

    # Interviews with no weight in any modeled type → assign to most common
    row_sums <- rowSums(gear_weight_matrix)
    zero_rows <- (row_sums == 0)
    if(any(zero_rows)) {
      most_common_idx <- which.max(colSums(gear_weight_matrix))
      gear_weight_matrix[zero_rows, most_common_idx] <- 1.0
      row_sums[zero_rows] <- 1.0
    }
    # Normalize rows to sum to 1
    gear_weight_matrix <- gear_weight_matrix / rowSums(gear_weight_matrix)
  }

  # --- Option B: the Stan model runs with G = 1 ------------------------------
  # crab_bss_gear_resolved.stan supports G > 1 structurally, but its only effort
  # observation touches g = 1 while E_sum / C_expected_sum sum over all g, so
  # raising G would add effort processes identified by their priors alone. Until
  # the gear-resolved reconstruction (Option A: effort shares in O[d,s,g] from
  # pi_gear_data, plus a multi-gear interview rule), collapse the Stan-facing gear
  # dimension to a single "All" category. The gear-composition diagnostics above
  # are retained for reporting.
  if(G_gear > 1) {
    cat(sprintf("  NOTE: %d gear types qualify (%s), but this Stan model runs with\n",
                G_gear, paste(gear_type_labels, collapse=", ")))
    cat("        G = 1. Collapsing to a single 'All' CPUE process. Gear-type catch\n")
    cat("        is reported by PE apportionment, not by the BSS. See Option A.\n")
    gear_type_labels <- "All"
    G_gear <- 1L
    gear_weight_matrix <- matrix(1.0, nrow = nrow(int_d), ncol = 1)
    colnames(gear_weight_matrix) <- "All"
  }

  cat(sprintf("  Gear types (%d): %s\n", G_gear, paste(gear_type_labels, collapse=", ")))
  cat(sprintf("  Effective N per gear type: %s\n",
    paste(sapply(1:G_gear, function(g) sprintf("%s=%.1f", gear_type_labels[g],
      colSums(gear_weight_matrix)[g])), collapse=", ")))

  # Report multi-gear interview fraction
  n_multi <- sum(int_d$n_gear_types_reported > 1)
  cat(sprintf("  Multi-gear interviews: %d of %d (%.0f%%)\n",
              n_multi, nrow(int_d), n_multi/max(nrow(int_d),1)*100))

  # =========================================================================
  # v5.1 EMPIRICAL pi_gear_data[P_n, 3, G_gear] with Laplace smoothing
  # =========================================================================
  # pi_gear is now DATA (not a Dirichlet parameter). Computed from interview
  # gear weights with Laplace smoothing (alpha=1) for each period × day_type.
  # Falls back to period-level, then sub-season-level if no interviews.

  n_day_types <- 3L  # 1=weekday, 2=weekend, 3=holiday
  alpha_smooth <- 1.0  # Laplace smoothing constant

  # Compute BSS period for each interview
  int_d$bss_period <- pvec[int_d$day_index]

  # Sub-season-level gear proportions (ultimate fallback)
  season_gear_sums <- colSums(gear_weight_matrix)
  season_pi <- (season_gear_sums + alpha_smooth) / (sum(season_gear_sums) + alpha_smooth * G_gear)

  pi_gear_data <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(P_n, n_day_types, G_gear))
  # v5.2 Fix B: Raw weighted counts for Dirichlet uncertainty propagation
  n_weighted_gear <- array(0, dim = c(P_n, n_day_types, G_gear))

  for(p in 1:P_n) {
    # Period-level proportions (intermediate fallback)
    mask_p <- (int_d$bss_period == p)
    if(any(mask_p)) {
      period_sums <- colSums(gear_weight_matrix[mask_p, , drop=FALSE])
      period_pi <- (period_sums + alpha_smooth) / (sum(period_sums) + alpha_smooth * G_gear)
    } else {
      period_pi <- season_pi
      period_sums <- season_gear_sums  # fallback raw counts for Dirichlet
    }

    for(dt in 1:n_day_types) {
      mask_pd <- mask_p & (int_d$day_type_idx == dt)
      if(any(mask_pd)) {
        dt_sums <- colSums(gear_weight_matrix[mask_pd, , drop=FALSE])
        pi_gear_data[p, dt, ] <- (dt_sums + alpha_smooth) / (sum(dt_sums) + alpha_smooth * G_gear)
        n_weighted_gear[p, dt, ] <- dt_sums  # raw counts for Stan Dirichlet
      } else {
        # Fallback: use period-level proportions and counts
        pi_gear_data[p, dt, ] <- period_pi
        n_weighted_gear[p, dt, ] <- if(any(mask_p)) period_sums else season_gear_sums
      }
    }
  }

  # --- Day type indices for Stan ---
  day_type_idx_vec <- days$day_type_idx

  # --- Sparse effort observation count ---
  n_gear <- if(is_shore) nrow(eff_d) else 0L
  n_trailer <- if(!is_shore) nrow(eff_d) else 0L
  n_effort_obs <- n_gear + n_trailer

  cat(sprintf("  Effort obs (NB-marginalized): %d (Gear_n + T_n)\n", n_effort_obs))

  # v5.3: Defensive check. Stan declares Gear_A_boat as int<lower=1>.
  # number_of_gear is stored as numeric (load_creel_data line ~305), so a
  # fractional value (e.g. 0.5) would pass the intA filter (> 0) but cast
  # to 0L and crash the Stan validator. Whole-number gear counts should
  # always hold in practice, but verify before building the data list.
  if(!is_shore && nrow(intA) > 0) {
    if(any(as.integer(intA$number_of_gear) < 1L)) {
      stop("prep_bss_crab: Gear_A_boat contains values < 1 after as.integer(). ",
           "Inspect intA$number_of_gear for non-integer or sub-1 entries.")
    }
  }

  list(
    D=D, G=G, S=S, P_n=P_n, period=pvec,
    w=days$day_type_num_weekend, holiday=days$day_type_num_holiday,
    # v5.3: Pots fish continuously while the trailer sits at the ramp,
    # so the daily fishing window for boats is 24 h. Shore crabbers
    # actively tend their gear, so the window remains day_length.
    # 5a/5b: shore L is the I/E-derived effective day length (days$L_mu). For shore
    # it is a PARAMETER (estimate_L = 1) with lognormal prior sd L_prior_sigma, so
    # the I/E prediction uncertainty propagates. Boats keep L fixed at 24 h.
    L_data        = if(is_shore) days$L_mu else rep(24.0, nrow(days)),
    estimate_L    = as.integer(is_shore),
    L_prior_sigma = if(is_shore) days$L_prior_sigma else rep(0.01, nrow(days)),

    # 5b: ingress/egress observation stream (shore only; IE_n = 0 for boats).
    IE_n             = IE_n,
    day_IE           = if(IE_n > 0) ie_match$day_index else integer(0),
    section_IE       = if(IE_n > 0) rep(1L, IE_n) else integer(0),
    IE_crabber_hours = if(IE_n > 0) ie_match$ie_crabber_hours else numeric(0),
    O=array(1.0, dim=c(D,S,G)),

    # Sparse effort observation count
    n_effort_obs = n_effort_obs,

    # Gear counts (shore only)
    Gear_n = n_gear,
    day_Gear = if(is_shore) eff_d$day_index else integer(0),
    section_Gear = if(is_shore) rep(1L,nrow(eff_d)) else integer(0),
    Gear_I = if(is_shore) as.integer(eff_d$count_quantity) else integer(0),

    # Trailer counts (boat only)
    T_n = n_trailer,
    day_T = if(!is_shore) eff_d$day_index else integer(0),
    section_T = if(!is_shore) rep(1L,nrow(eff_d)) else integer(0),
    T_I = if(!is_shore) as.integer(eff_d$count_quantity) else integer(0),

    # Direct crabber counts (reserved)
    Crab_n=0L, day_Crab=integer(0), section_Crab=integer(0),
    Crab_I=integer(0), p_I_crab=1.0,

    # Interview CPUE data (with gear weights — v5.1 Issue 4)
    # v5.3: For boats, h is gear-hours to match lambda_E (gear in water).
    # For shore, h remains crabber-hours.
    IntC=nrow(int_d), day_IntC=int_d$day_index, gear_IntC=rep(1L,nrow(int_d)),
    section_IntC=rep(1L,nrow(int_d)), c=as.integer(int_d$fish_count),
    h = if(is_shore) int_d$fishing_time_total else int_d$gear_time_total,

    # Gear-per-crabber expansion (shore only)
    IntA_gear = if(is_shore) nrow(intA) else 0L,
    Gear_A = if(is_shore) as.integer(intA$number_of_gear) else integer(0),
    A_A_gear = if(is_shore) as.integer(intA$angler_count) else integer(0),

    # Gear-per-boat-group expansion (boat only)
    # v5.3: Replaces T_A_int / A_A_trailer. The Stan model learns R_G_boat
    # (mean gear deployed per boat group) directly from observed
    # number_of_gear via Gear_A_boat[a] ~ Poisson(R_G_boat). The intA
    # filter already enforces number_of_gear > 0 and angler_count > 0,
    # which satisfies the Stan constraint int<lower=1> Gear_A_boat[].
    IntA_trailer = if(!is_shore) nrow(intA) else 0L,
    Gear_A_boat  = if(!is_shore) as.integer(intA$number_of_gear) else integer(0),

    # R_G estimation control (v5.1 Issue 5)
    # NOTE: estimate_R_G / R_G_fixed are not declared in the Stan model; R_G is
    # always a parameter with a proper lognormal prior. Retained for Option A.
    estimate_R_G = as.integer(is_shore),
    R_G_fixed = params$R_G_fixed_boat,

    # Hyperparameters (v5.1 Issue 7: Cauchy scales tightened to 2)
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_eps_E=2, value_cauchyDF_sigma_r_E=2,
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_eps_C=2, value_cauchyDF_sigma_r_C=2,
    value_betashape_phi_E_scaled=2, value_betashape_phi_C_scaled=2,
    value_normal_sigma_B1=1,
    value_normal_sigma_B2=1,
    value_normal_sigma_B1_C=1,
    use_B1_C = as.integer(isTRUE(params$estimate_B1_C)),
    value_normal_mu_mu_C=log(0.5), value_normal_sigma_mu_C=2,
    value_normal_mu_mu_E=if(is_shore) log(25) else log(10),
    value_normal_sigma_mu_E=2,
    value_cauchyDF_sigma_mu_C=2, value_cauchyDF_sigma_mu_E=2,

    # Metadata for output (not passed to Stan)
    .gear_type_labels = gear_type_labels,
    .ar_resolution    = ar_resolution
  )
}


# ===========================================================================
# 6. COMMERCIAL/CHARTER CENSUS ESTIMATION
# ===========================================================================

estimate_comm_charter <- function(dwg, params) {
  cat("\n--- Commercial/Charter Census Estimation (Stratified) ---\n")

  census_start <- as.Date(params$census_start_date)
  census_end   <- as.Date(params$census_end_date)

  tally <- dwg$comm_tally |>
    filter(between(date, census_start, census_end))

  comm_int <- dwg$interview |>
    filter(population == "comm_charter",
           between(event_date, census_start, census_end))

  if(nrow(comm_int) == 0 || nrow(tally) == 0) {
    cat("  No commercial/charter data available.\n")
    result <- list(effort_total=0, Dungeness_Kept=0)
    if(params$estimate_red_rock) result$Red_Rock_Kept <- 0
    return(result)
  }

  mean_dung_per_vessel <- sum(comm_int$dungeness_kept) / nrow(comm_int)
  rr_str <- ""
  if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
    mean_rr_per_vessel <- sum(comm_int$red_rock_kept) / nrow(comm_int)
    rr_str <- sprintf(", Mean Red Rock/vessel: %.1f", mean_rr_per_vessel)
  }

  cat(sprintf("  Tally days: %d, Interviews: %d\n", nrow(tally), nrow(comm_int)))
  cat(sprintf("  Mean Dungeness/vessel: %.1f%s\n", mean_dung_per_vessel, rr_str))

  daily_est <- tally |>
    mutate(
      total_comm_charter = commercial_tally + charter_tally,
      est_dung = total_comm_charter * mean_dung_per_vessel,
      day_of_week = weekdays(date),
      day_type = case_when(
        date %in% crabbing_holiday_dates ~ "weekend",
        day_of_week %in% params$days_wkend ~ "weekend",
        TRUE ~ "weekday"
      )
    )

  if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
    daily_est <- daily_est |> mutate(est_rr = total_comm_charter * mean_rr_per_vessel)
  }

  census_calendar <- tibble(
    date = seq.Date(census_start, census_end, by = "day"),
    day_of_week = weekdays(date),
    day_type = case_when(
      date %in% crabbing_holiday_dates ~ "weekend",
      day_of_week %in% params$days_wkend ~ "weekend",
      TRUE ~ "weekday"
    )
  )

  total_by_type <- census_calendar |> count(day_type, name = "n_total_days")
  sampled_by_type <- daily_est |> count(day_type, name = "n_sampled_days")

  strat <- total_by_type |>
    left_join(sampled_by_type, by = "day_type") |>
    mutate(n_sampled_days = replace_na(n_sampled_days, 0))

  strat_harvest <- daily_est |>
    group_by(day_type) |>
    summarise(
      mean_daily_dung = mean(est_dung),
      mean_daily_vessels = mean(total_comm_charter),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
    strat_harvest_rr <- daily_est |>
      group_by(day_type) |>
      summarise(mean_daily_rr = mean(est_rr), .groups = "drop")
    strat_harvest <- strat_harvest |> left_join(strat_harvest_rr, by = "day_type")
  }

  strat <- strat |>
    left_join(strat_harvest, by = "day_type") |>
    mutate(
      est_total_dung = mean_daily_dung * n_total_days,
      est_total_vessels = mean_daily_vessels * n_total_days
    )

  cat("\n  Stratified expansion by day type:\n")
  cat(sprintf("    %-10s  Sampled  Total  Mean/day  Expanded\n", "Day Type"))
  for(i in 1:nrow(strat)) {
    cat(sprintf("    %-10s  %5d    %5d  %7.1f   %8.0f\n",
                strat$day_type[i], strat$n_sampled_days[i], strat$n_total_days[i],
                strat$mean_daily_dung[i], strat$est_total_dung[i]))
  }

  total_dung <- sum(strat$est_total_dung)
  total_vessels <- sum(strat$est_total_vessels)

  result <- list(
    Dungeness_Kept = total_dung,
    effort_total = total_vessels,
    daily_est = daily_est,
    strat_detail = strat
  )

  if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
    strat <- strat |>
      left_join(daily_est |> group_by(day_type) |>
        summarise(mean_daily_rr = mean(est_rr), .groups = "drop"), by = "day_type") |>
      mutate(est_total_rr = mean_daily_rr * n_total_days)
    result$Red_Rock_Kept <- sum(strat$est_total_rr)
  }

  dung_str <- format(round(total_dung), big.mark=",")
  rr_out <- if(params$estimate_red_rock) sprintf(", Red Rock: %s", format(round(result$Red_Rock_Kept),big.mark=",")) else ""
  cat(sprintf("\n  Est Dungeness (stratified): %s%s\n", dung_str, rr_out))

  return(result)
}


# ===========================================================================
# 7. RUN EVERYTHING
# ===========================================================================

# ---- 7.0 LOAD DATA ----
timer_start("1_load_data")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n LOADING DATA\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  LOADING DATA
##  ============================================================
dwg <- fetch_crab_data_v2(params)
## Reading data...
##   Grays Harbor interviews by population:
##     comm_charter: 185 
##     private_boat: 422 
##     shore: 3752 
## 
##   Shore effort obs: 365 (197 days)
##   Boat effort obs: 240 (186 days)
##   Commercial tally days: 47
# --- Ingress/egress data + L_effective model (shared with the pooled driver) ---
# fetch_ie_data() and estimate_L_effective() live in 03_R_functions/bss_day_length.R.
# L_eff_model is NULL when I/E data are absent or fewer than 3 shore days exist;
# prep_days_crab() then falls back to civil twilight and warns.
ie_data <- fetch_ie_data(params)
## 
##   Reading I/E data...
##   I/E survey days: 30 shore (WDF20), 2 boat (WBL)
##   Date range: 2023-08-17 to 2026-03-05
##   Shore L_effective: mean=5.3 hrs (range 2.7–7.0)
L_eff_model <- NULL
if(isTRUE(params$use_ie_day_length) && nrow(ie_data) > 0) {
  L_eff_model <- estimate_L_effective(ie_data, params$pot_open_date, params)
  if(!is.null(L_eff_model) && !is.null(L_eff_model$detail)) {
    write.csv(L_eff_model$detail,
              file.path(output_dir, "L_effective_ie_detail.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  }
}
## 
##   Fitting L_effective regression from historical I/E data...
##   I/E observations for regression: 30
##   L_effective regression summary:
##     R² = 0.183, residual SE = 0.201 (log scale)
##     Coefficients:
##       (Intercept): 1.6579
##       poly(yday, 2)1: 0.4327
##       poly(yday, 2)2: -0.1112
##       day_type_groupweekend: -0.0453
## 
##   Predicted L_effective range: 4.5–5.7 hrs
##   Prediction uncertainty (sigma on log scale): 0.21–0.22
timer_stop("1_load_data")
##   [TIMER] 1_load_data: 0.0 min
# ---- 7.1 DIAGNOSTIC PLOTS ----
timer_start("2_diagnostic_plots")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n DIAGNOSTIC PLOTS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  DIAGNOSTIC PLOTS
##  ============================================================
days_full <- prep_days_crab(params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
                            params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                            params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
## Warning: Calling `case_when()` with size 1 LHS inputs and size >1 RHS inputs was deprecated in dplyr 1.2.0.
## ℹ This `case_when()` statement can result in subtle silent bugs and is very inefficient.
## 
##   Please use a series of if statements instead:
## 
##   ```
##   # Previously
##   case_when(scalar_lhs1 ~ rhs1, scalar_lhs2 ~ rhs2, .default = default)
## 
##   # Now
##   if (scalar_lhs1) {
##     rhs1
##   } else if (scalar_lhs2) {
##     rhs2
##   } else {
##     default
##   }
##   ```
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was generated.
# Plot 1: Shore gear count timeseries
p_shore_effort <- dwg$shore_effort |>
  group_by(event_date) |>
  summarise(total_gear = sum(count_quantity), n_counts = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(days_full |> select(event_date, day_type), by = "event_date") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = event_date, y = total_gear, color = day_type, size = n_counts)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(params$pot_open_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date(params$pot_open_date) + 5, y = Inf, label = "Pots open",
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "red") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1,3), breaks = 1:3, name = "Counts/day") +
  labs(title = "Shore Crabbers: Daily Gear Count (Docks F20 + F17-21)",
       subtitle = "Point size = count sequences per day",
       x = "Date", y = "Total gear count", color = "Day type") +
  theme_bw()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_shore_effort_timeseries.png"), p_shore_effort, width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_shore_effort)

# Plot 2: Boat trailer count timeseries
p_boat_effort <- dwg$boat_effort |>
  group_by(event_date) |>
  summarise(total_trailers = sum(count_quantity), n_counts = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(days_full |> select(event_date, day_type), by = "event_date") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = event_date, y = total_trailers, color = day_type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(params$pot_open_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Private Boat Crabbers: Daily Trailer Count (Boat Launch)",
       x = "Date", y = "Total trailer count", color = "Day type") +
  theme_bw()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_boat_effort_timeseries.png"), p_boat_effort, width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_boat_effort)

# Plot 3: CPUE timeseries by population
cpue_data <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(fishing_time_total > 0, dungeness_kept >= 0) |>
  mutate(cpue = dungeness_kept / fishing_time_total) |>
  group_by(event_date, population) |>
  summarise(mean_cpue = mean(cpue), n_int = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(days_full |> select(event_date, day_type), by = "event_date") |>
  filter(population %in% c("shore", "private_boat"))

p_cpue <- cpue_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = event_date, y = mean_cpue, color = day_type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = TRUE, color = "grey30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(params$pot_open_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~population, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(population = c(shore = "Shore Crabbers", private_boat = "Private Boat Crabbers"))) +
  labs(title = "Dungeness CPUE by Population",
       x = "Date", y = "Mean CPUE (crab/crabber-hr)", color = "Day type") +
  theme_bw()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_cpue_timeseries.png"), p_cpue, width = 10, height = 8)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
print(p_cpue)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Plot 4: Gear counts by month and day type (boxplot)
p_effort_month <- dwg$shore_effort |>
  group_by(event_date) |>
  summarise(total_gear = sum(count_quantity), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(days_full |> select(event_date, day_type, month, year), by = "event_date") |>
  mutate(month_label = factor(format(event_date, "%Y-%m"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month_label, y = total_gear, fill = day_type)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Shore Gear Counts by Month and Day Type",
       x = "Month", y = "Daily gear count", fill = "Day type") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_effort_by_month.png"), p_effort_month, width = 12, height = 6)
print(p_effort_month)

# Plot 5: Commercial/charter vessel tally
if(nrow(dwg$comm_tally) > 0) {
  p_comm <- dwg$comm_tally |>
    pivot_longer(cols = c(commercial_tally, charter_tally, private_tally),
                 names_to = "vessel_type", values_to = "count") |>
    mutate(vessel_type = str_remove(vessel_type, "_tally") |> str_to_title()) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = date, y = count, fill = vessel_type)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(params$commercial_opener), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = as.Date(params$commercial_opener) + 2, y = Inf,
             label = "Commercial opener", hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "red") +
    labs(title = "Westport Marina: Daily Vessel Tally",
         x = "Date", y = "Vessels", fill = "Type") +
    theme_bw()
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_commercial_tally.png"), p_comm, width = 10, height = 5)
  print(p_comm)
}

cat("  Diagnostic plots saved.\n")
##   Diagnostic plots saved.
timer_stop("2_diagnostic_plots")
##   [TIMER] 2_diagnostic_plots: 0.1 min
# ---- 7.2 PE ESTIMATES ----
timer_start("3_PE_estimates")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n PE ESTIMATES\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  PE ESTIMATES
##  ============================================================
pe_all <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                              params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)
    pe_all[[label]] <- run_pe(summ_ss, days_ss, params, label)
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
##   PE shore_ring_net_only: Effort=23,351 hrs, Dung=13,403
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   PE shore_all_gear: Effort=116,220 hrs, Dung=36,578
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   PE private_boat_ring_net_only: Effort=1,687 hrs, Dung=945
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
##   PE private_boat_all_gear: Effort=34,668 hrs, Dung=11,162
# ---- 7.3 PE PLOTS ----
cat("\n  Generating PE plots...\n")
## 
##   Generating PE plots...
pe_daily_all <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                              params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

    de <- summ_ss$effort_index |>
      filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
      group_by(event_date, section_num) |>
      summarise(mean_count = mean(count_quantity), .groups = "drop") |>
      mutate(est_crabbers = mean_count * summ_ss$crabbers_per_gear) |>
      left_join(days_ss |> select(event_date, day_type, day_length), by = "event_date") |>
      mutate(est_daily_crabber_hours = est_crabbers * day_length,
             population = pop, subseason = ss$name)
    pe_daily_all[[label]] <- de
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
pe_daily_df <- bind_rows(pe_daily_all)

p_pe_effort <- pe_daily_df |>
  mutate(pop_label = case_when(
    population == "shore" ~ "Shore Crabbers",
    population == "private_boat" ~ "Private Boat Crabbers")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = event_date, y = est_daily_crabber_hours, color = day_type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = TRUE, color = "grey30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(params$pot_open_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~pop_label, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "PE: Estimated Daily Crabber-Hours by Population",
       x = "Date", y = "Estimated crabber-hours", color = "Day type") +
  theme_bw()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_pe_daily_effort.png"), p_pe_effort, width = 10, height = 8)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
print(p_pe_effort)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

timer_stop("3_PE_estimates")
##   [TIMER] 3_PE_estimates: 0.0 min
# ---- 7.4 COMMERCIAL/CHARTER ----
timer_start("4_commercial_charter")
pe_all$comm_charter <- estimate_comm_charter(dwg, params)
## 
## --- Commercial/Charter Census Estimation (Stratified) ---
##   Tally days: 47, Interviews: 160
##   Mean Dungeness/vessel: 42.5
## 
##   Stratified expansion by day type:
##     Day Type    Sampled  Total  Mean/day  Expanded
##     weekday        23       38    193.8       7365
##     weekend        24       32    145.1       4642
## 
##   Est Dungeness (stratified): 12,007
timer_stop("4_commercial_charter")
##   [TIMER] 4_commercial_charter: 0.0 min
# ---- 7.5 PE PORT TOTALS ----
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n PE PORT TOTALS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  PE PORT TOTALS
##  ============================================================
component_names <- c("Shore (ring-net)","Shore (all-gear)","Private Boat (ring-net)",
                     "Private Boat (all-gear)","Commercial/Charter","PORT TOTAL")
component_keys <- c("shore_ring_net_only","shore_all_gear",
                    "private_boat_ring_net_only","private_boat_all_gear","comm_charter")

pe_summary <- tibble(
  Component = component_names,
  Effort_hrs = c(sapply(component_keys, function(k) pe_all[[k]]$effort_total), NA),
  Dungeness = c(sapply(component_keys, function(k) pe_all[[k]]$Dungeness_Kept), NA)
)
pe_summary$Effort_hrs[6] <- sum(pe_summary$Effort_hrs[1:5], na.rm=TRUE)
pe_summary$Dungeness[6] <- sum(pe_summary$Dungeness[1:5], na.rm=TRUE)

if(params$estimate_red_rock) {
  pe_summary$Red_Rock <- c(
    sapply(component_keys, function(k) pe_all[[k]]$Red_Rock_Kept %||% 0), NA)
  pe_summary$Red_Rock[6] <- sum(pe_summary$Red_Rock[1:5], na.rm=TRUE)
}

print(pe_summary |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.))))
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   Component               Effort_hrs Dungeness
##   <chr>                        <dbl>     <dbl>
## 1 Shore (ring-net)             23351     13403
## 2 Shore (all-gear)            116220     36578
## 3 Private Boat (ring-net)       1687       945
## 4 Private Boat (all-gear)      34668     11162
## 5 Commercial/Charter             283     12007
## 6 PORT TOTAL                  176208     74095
write.csv(pe_summary, file.path(output_dir, "pe_port_summary.csv"), row.names=FALSE)


# ---- 7.6 BSS MODEL RUNS ----
timer_start("5_BSS_model_runs")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n BSS MODEL RUNS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  BSS MODEL RUNS
##  ============================================================
bss_catch_groups <- catch_groups
cat(sprintf("Running BSS for: %s\n", paste(bss_catch_groups, collapse=", ")))
## Running BSS for: Dungeness_Kept
bss_all <- list()
ar_resolutions <- list()   # AR resolution actually used, per fit (for the gate report)
for(cg in bss_catch_groups) {
  for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
    for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
      label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_", cg)
      cat(sprintf("\n--- BSS: %s ---\n", label))

      days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                                params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
      summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

      n_int <- nrow(summ_ss$interview)
      n_eff <- nrow(summ_ss$effort_index)

      if(n_eff < 5 || n_int < params$bss_min_interviews) {
        cat(sprintf("  Insufficient data for BSS (%d effort, %d interviews < %d min), using PE only.\n",
                    n_eff, n_int, params$bss_min_interviews))
        bss_all[[label]] <- list(C_sum=NA, E_sum=NA, pe_fallback=TRUE,
                                 population=pop, subseason=ss$name, catch_group=cg)
        next
      }

      # v5.1: Pass sub-season-specific period type
      bss_data <- prep_bss_crab(days_ss, summ_ss, cg, params, pop, ss$period_bss,
                                gear_exclude = ss$gear_exclude %||% character(0),
                                ie_data = ie_data)
      ar_resolutions[[label]] <- bss_data$.ar_resolution

      is_shore <- (pop == "shore")
      is_allgear <- (ss$name == "all_gear")
      is_ringnet <- (ss$name == "ring_net_only")

      # Per-fit sampler tuning: shore all-gear is the most complex posterior
      # (4 gear types, 10 monthly periods); ring-net fits are data-sparse
      # and benefit from tighter adaptation. All other fits use defaults.
      if(is_shore && is_allgear) {
        fit_iter    <- params$bss_iter_shore_allgear
        fit_warmup  <- params$bss_warmup_shore_allgear
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_treedepth_shore_allgear %||% params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_delta_shore_allgear %||% params$bss_adapt_delta_default
      } else if(is_ringnet) {
        fit_iter    <- params$bss_iter_ringnet %||% params$bss_iter_default
        fit_warmup  <- params$bss_warmup_ringnet %||% params$bss_warmup_default
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_treedepth_ringnet %||% params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_delta_ringnet %||% params$bss_adapt_delta_default
      } else {
        fit_iter    <- params$bss_iter_default
        fit_warmup  <- params$bss_warmup_default
        fit_treedep <- params$bss_max_treedepth_default
        fit_delta   <- params$bss_adapt_delta_default
      }

      cat(sprintf("  Stan settings: iter=%d, warmup=%d, max_treedepth=%d, adapt_delta=%.2f\n",
                  fit_iter, fit_warmup, fit_treedep, fit_delta))

      timer_start(paste0("BSS_", label))
      fit <- rstan::stan(
        file = here("02_stan_models", params$bss_model_file),
        data = bss_data,
        chains = params$bss_chains, iter = fit_iter, warmup = fit_warmup,
        control = list(adapt_delta = fit_delta, max_treedepth = fit_treedep),
        seed = params$bss_seed,   # was missing: gear-resolved fits were not reproducible
        cores = params$bss_cores
      )
      timer_stop(paste0("BSS_", label))

      # --- Extract and save BSS summary ---
      # C_sum_gear is the predictive season total per gear. With G = 1 it is a
      # single 'All' column and sum(C_sum_gear) == C_sum. The Dirichlet pi_gear
      # machinery described in older comments is not present in the Stan model.
      bss_summ <- summary(fit, pars=c("C_sum","E_sum","C_sum_gear","B1","B2",
                                       "r_C_gear_out","sigma_eps_C_gear_out"))$summary
      cat("  BSS Summary:\n"); print(bss_summ)
      write.csv(as.data.frame(bss_summ), file.path(output_dir, paste0("bss_summary_",label,".csv")))

      C_draws <- rstan::extract(fit, "C_sum")$C_sum
      E_draws <- rstan::extract(fit, "E_sum")$E_sum

      C_gear_draws <- rstan::extract(fit, "C_sum_gear")$C_sum_gear  # [draws, G_gear]
      gear_labels <- bss_data$.gear_type_labels

      bss_all[[label]] <- list(
        C_sum = quantile(C_draws, c(0.025,0.5,0.975)),
        E_sum = quantile(E_draws, c(0.025,0.5,0.975)),
        C_draws = C_draws, E_draws = E_draws,
        C_gear_draws = C_gear_draws,
        gear_type_labels = gear_labels,
        fit = fit, pe_fallback = FALSE,
        # Carried for the shared diagnostics in 03_R_functions (PPC, effort
        # overdispersion decomposition, O1-O13, PSIS-LOO). Without these the
        # writers silently skip, so they are stored on the fit entry.
        bss_data = bss_data, days_ss = days_ss,
        population = pop, subseason = ss$name, catch_group = cg
      )

      # --- BSS PLOTS for this fit ---

      # Daily effort posteriors (ribbon plot)
      E_bss <- rstan::extract(fit, "E")$E  # [draws, S, D, G]
      E_daily <- tibble(
        event_date = days_ss$event_date,
        median = apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, median),
        lo95 = apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, quantile, 0.025),
        hi95 = apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, quantile, 0.975)
      )

      p_bss_eff <- E_daily |>
        left_join(days_ss |> select(event_date, day_type), by = "event_date") |>
        ggplot(aes(x = event_date)) +
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +
        geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "steelblue") +
        labs(title = sprintf("BSS Daily Effort: %s", label),
             subtitle = "Median with 95% credible interval",
             x = "Date", y = "Crabber-hours") +
        theme_bw()
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_bss_effort_", label, ".png")),
             p_bss_eff, width = 10, height = 6)
      print(p_bss_eff)

      # Daily catch posteriors
      # v5.2: C_total is now the expected value (smooth trajectory, no Poisson noise).
      # Uses C_total for trajectory plots; C_gear_pred used for season totals.
      C_total_bss <- rstan::extract(fit, "C_total")$C_total  # [draws, D]
      C_daily <- tibble(
        event_date = days_ss$event_date,
        median = apply(C_total_bss, 2, median),
        lo95 = apply(C_total_bss, 2, quantile, 0.025),
        hi95 = apply(C_total_bss, 2, quantile, 0.975)
      )

      p_bss_catch <- C_daily |>
        ggplot(aes(x = event_date)) +
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkgreen") +
        geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkgreen") +
        labs(title = sprintf("BSS Daily Catch (%s): %s", cg, label),
             subtitle = "Median with 95% credible interval",
             x = "Date", y = "Dungeness crab caught") +
        theme_bw()
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_bss_catch_", label, ".png")),
             p_bss_catch, width = 10, height = 6)
      print(p_bss_catch)

      # Season total posterior densities
      p_bss_post <- tibble(C_sum = C_draws, E_sum = E_draws) |>
        pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "param", values_to = "value") |>
        ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
        geom_density(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.4) +
        facet_wrap(~param, scales = "free", labeller = labeller(
          param = c(C_sum = paste("Catch", cg), E_sum = "Effort (crabber-hrs)")
        )) +
        labs(title = sprintf("BSS Posteriors: %s", label), x = "Value", y = "Density") +
        theme_bw()
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("plot_bss_posteriors_", label, ".png")),
             p_bss_post, width = 12, height = 5)
      print(p_bss_post)

      # Save daily CSVs
      write.csv(E_daily, file.path(output_dir, paste0("bss_daily_effort_", label, ".csv")), row.names=FALSE)
      write.csv(C_daily, file.path(output_dir, paste0("bss_daily_catch_", label, ".csv")), row.names=FALSE)

    }
  }
}
## 
## --- BSS: shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept ---
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
##   AR resolution: biweekly [fixed] (P_n=6) | coverage=68% (52/76 days), 4.7 obs/week
##   Trip completion: 627 complete, 229 incomplete (27%), 0 missing status
##   CPUE: complete=0.656, incomplete=0.550 (bias=-16.2%)
##   Incomplete trip filter ON: 856 → 627 interviews (removed 229)
##   I/E observations: 2 days (crabber-hrs 88-158)
##   Regulatory exclusion: Pot removed (prohibited this sub-season)
##   NOTE: 3 gear types qualify (Ring Net, Snare, Trap), but this Stan model runs with
##         G = 1. Collapsing to a single 'All' CPUE process. Gear-type catch
##         is reported by PE apportionment, not by the BSS. See Option A.
##   Gear types (1): All
##   Effective N per gear type: All=627.0
##   Multi-gear interviews: 149 of 627 (24%)
##   Effort obs (NB-marginalized): 52 (Gear_n + T_n)
##   Stan settings: iter=4000, warmup=2000, max_treedepth=12, adapt_delta=0.95
## Warning: There were 7818 divergent transitions after warmup. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup
## to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them.
## Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems
## Warning: The largest R-hat is NA, indicating chains have not mixed.
