## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#r-hat
## Warning: Bulk Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior means and medians may be unreliable.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#bulk-ess
## Warning: Tail Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior variances and tail quantiles may be unreliable.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess
##   [TIMER] BSS_shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept: 10.7 min
##   BSS Summary:
##                                 mean     se_mean          sd          2.5%           25%          50%          75%        97.5%     n_eff     Rhat
## C_sum                   6.864077e+03 86.81621081 676.7509704 5758.97500000  6.361000e+03 6.803000e+03 7.296000e+03 8.318050e+03  60.76537 1.061560
## E_sum                   1.112511e+04 28.62933856 891.3261973 9561.37824510  1.056939e+04 1.098399e+04 1.165441e+04 1.310223e+04 969.28326 1.018260
## C_sum_gear[1]           6.864077e+03 86.81621081 676.7509704 5758.97500000  6.361000e+03 6.803000e+03 7.296000e+03 8.318050e+03  60.76537 1.061560
## B1                      5.200870e-01  0.01069931   0.1388970    0.23441504  4.236518e-01 5.330647e-01 6.107716e-01 7.842818e-01 168.52882 1.037846
## B2                      7.794213e-02  0.02383059   0.4887418   -0.86766798 -2.583268e-01 1.160280e-01 3.444018e-01 1.099956e+00 420.61962 1.020601
## r_C_gear_out[1]         1.358447e+00  0.01161877   0.1221591    1.15089898  1.269480e+00 1.338951e+00 1.437627e+00 1.626429e+00 110.54301 1.049434
## sigma_eps_C_gear_out[1] 1.599008e-01  0.00890338   0.1117964    0.01744658  8.452193e-02 1.400999e-01 2.169033e-01 4.280288e-01 157.66892 1.057465

## 
## --- BSS: shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept ---
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   AR resolution: monthly [fixed] (P_n=10) | coverage=50% (145/289 days), 3.3 obs/week
##   Trip completion: 1646 complete, 1091 incomplete (40%), 5 missing status
##   CPUE: complete=0.384, incomplete=0.331 (bias=-13.8%)
##   Incomplete trip filter ON: 2742 → 1651 interviews (removed 1091)
##   I/E observations: 4 days (crabber-hrs 24-331)
##   NOTE: 4 gear types qualify (Pot, Ring Net, Snare, Trap), but this Stan model runs with
##         G = 1. Collapsing to a single 'All' CPUE process. Gear-type catch
##         is reported by PE apportionment, not by the BSS. See Option A.
##   Gear types (1): All
##   Effective N per gear type: All=1651.0
##   Multi-gear interviews: 531 of 1651 (32%)
##   Effort obs (NB-marginalized): 311 (Gear_n + T_n)
##   Stan settings: iter=4000, warmup=2000, max_treedepth=14, adapt_delta=0.95
## recompiling to avoid crashing R session
## Warning: There were 7807 divergent transitions after warmup. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup
## to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them.
## Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems
## Warning: The largest R-hat is NA, indicating chains have not mixed.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#r-hat
## Warning: Bulk Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior means and medians may be unreliable.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#bulk-ess
## Warning: Tail Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior variances and tail quantiles may be unreliable.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess
##   [TIMER] BSS_shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 19.8 min
##   BSS Summary:
##                                 mean      se_mean           sd         2.5%          25%          50%          75%        97.5%     n_eff     Rhat
## C_sum                   1.972647e+04 3.333028e+01 1.192662e+03 1.751598e+04 1.889700e+04 1.969350e+04 2.050425e+04 2.217605e+04 1280.4334 1.001306
## E_sum                   5.382520e+04 1.242121e+02 2.382369e+03 4.936549e+04 5.217670e+04 5.375181e+04 5.540130e+04 5.881038e+04  367.8667 1.003832
## C_sum_gear[1]           1.972647e+04 3.333028e+01 1.192662e+03 1.751598e+04 1.889700e+04 1.969350e+04 2.050425e+04 2.217605e+04 1280.4334 1.001306
## B1                      7.676954e-01 3.345107e-03 6.654300e-02 6.347669e-01 7.227727e-01 7.682016e-01 8.147119e-01 8.910725e-01  395.7169 1.010645
## B2                      3.853122e-01 6.665318e-03 1.765971e-01 3.686415e-02 2.727878e-01 3.714265e-01 5.049667e-01 7.470877e-01  701.9814 1.004061
## r_C_gear_out[1]         7.482383e-01 1.639599e-03 4.129216e-02 6.666381e-01 7.204701e-01 7.491722e-01 7.742616e-01 8.312558e-01  634.2493 1.005990
## sigma_eps_C_gear_out[1] 3.525521e-01 5.719454e-03 1.057299e-01 1.992926e-01 2.762311e-01 3.342790e-01 4.099417e-01 5.984521e-01  341.7324 1.010158

## 
## --- BSS: private_boat_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept ---
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   Insufficient data for BSS (44 effort, 17 interviews < 20 min), using PE only.
## 
## --- BSS: private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept ---
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
##   AR resolution: monthly [fixed] (P_n=10) | coverage=49% (143/289 days), 3.2 obs/week
##   Trip completion: 131 complete, 14 incomplete (10%), 0 missing status
##   CPUE: complete=1.135, incomplete=0.781 (bias=-31.1%)
##   Incomplete trip filter ON: 145 → 131 interviews (removed 14)
##   I/E observations: 0
##   Boat gear-hours filter: 131 → 131 interviews (require non-NA gear_time_total)
##   Insufficient gear type diversity — using single 'All' category
##   Gear types (1): All
##   Effective N per gear type: All=131.0
##   Multi-gear interviews: 8 of 131 (6%)
##   Effort obs (NB-marginalized): 195 (Gear_n + T_n)
##   Stan settings: iter=2000, warmup=1000, max_treedepth=10, adapt_delta=0.90
## recompiling to avoid crashing R session
## Warning: There were 109 divergent transitions after warmup. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup
## to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them.
## Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems
##   [TIMER] BSS_private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 4.0 min
##   BSS Summary:
##                                 mean      se_mean           sd          2.5%          25%          50%          75%        97.5%    n_eff      Rhat
## C_sum                   1.610615e+05 8.692146e+02 3.923491e+04  1.007165e+05 1.327455e+05 1.550380e+05 1.840238e+05 2.509035e+05 2037.470 1.0011390
## E_sum                   2.859471e+05 5.162507e+02 3.277778e+04  2.290676e+05 2.632741e+05 2.833549e+05 3.057150e+05 3.583681e+05 4031.231 0.9996177
## C_sum_gear[1]           1.610615e+05 8.692146e+02 3.923491e+04  1.007165e+05 1.327455e+05 1.550380e+05 1.840238e+05 2.509035e+05 2037.470 1.0011390
## B1                      2.623622e-01 2.670998e-03 1.603463e-01 -4.673233e-02 1.554263e-01 2.629760e-01 3.695838e-01 5.742470e-01 3603.882 0.9998347
## B2                      5.400308e-01 7.630370e-03 3.976729e-01 -2.182943e-01 2.758503e-01 5.253571e-01 7.892862e-01 1.358617e+00 2716.194 1.0010169
## r_C_gear_out[1]         8.170657e-01 1.725364e-03 1.061259e-01  6.250021e-01 7.416191e-01 8.097100e-01 8.864021e-01 1.040477e+00 3783.389 0.9994991
## sigma_eps_C_gear_out[1] 4.299590e-01 6.249657e-03 2.327696e-01  8.520975e-02 2.748440e-01 3.910452e-01 5.441645e-01 1.003482e+00 1387.203 1.0019459

timer_stop("5_BSS_model_runs")
##   [TIMER] 5_BSS_model_runs: 35.3 min
# ---- 7.6b CONVERGENCE GATE (B1.8, scale-aware; shared in 03_R_functions) ----
# Decide PE-vs-BSS per fit BEFORE any reported total is assembled. bss_compute_gate
# and bss_use_pe_for live in 03_R_functions/bss_convergence_gate.R (shared with the
# pooled track). A fit reports BSS only if it passes the gate; otherwise the
# reported-estimate sections below fall back to PE. Diagnostic sections still key
# off pe_fallback so a fitted-but-gate-failed component keeps its per-fit outputs.
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CONVERGENCE GATE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  CONVERGENCE GATE
##  ============================================================
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  gi <- bss_compute_gate(
    bss_all[[label]], label,
    catch_par        = "C_expected_sum",
    effort_par       = "E_sum",
    max_impact_sd    = params$max_divergence_total_impact_sd %||% 0.10,
    max_div_fraction = params$max_divergence_fraction %||% 0.05,
    ar_resolution    = ar_resolutions[[label]] %||% NA_character_
  )
  bss_all[[label]]$gate_info <- gi
  bss_all[[label]]$use_bss   <- isTRUE(gi$pass_convergence)
}
##   shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept: 7818/8000 divergent (97.7%); impact C/E = 0.3382/0.2824 SD; level-distortion C/E = 3.3%/2.3%
##   WARNING: shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept - divergence fraction 97.7% exceeds backstop 5%; forcing PE.
##   shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 7807/8000 divergent (97.6%); impact C/E = 0.0718/0.0012 SD; level-distortion C/E = 0.4%/0.0%
##   WARNING: shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept - divergence fraction 97.6% exceeds backstop 5%; forcing PE.
##   private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 109/4000 divergent (2.7%); impact C/E = 0.0022/0.0084 SD; level-distortion C/E = 4.2%/1.5%
# ---- 7.7 COMBINED BSS PORT TOTAL ----
timer_start("6_outputs")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n COMBINED RESULTS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  COMBINED RESULTS
##  ============================================================
n_draws_max <- max(sapply(bss_all, function(b) {
  if(bss_use_pe_for(b)) return(0)   # B1.8: a gate-failed fit contributes no BSS draws
  length(b$C_draws)
}))
if(n_draws_max == 0) n_draws_max <- params$bss_chains * (params$bss_iter_default - params$bss_warmup_default)

for(cg in bss_catch_groups) {
  bss_C_total <- rep(0, n_draws_max)
  bss_E_total <- rep(0, n_draws_max)

  for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
    for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
      label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_", cg)
      pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
      b <- bss_all[[label]]

      if(bss_use_pe_for(b)) {   # B1.8: PE if insufficient data OR failed the gate
        bss_C_total <- bss_C_total + (pe_all[[pe_label]][[cg]] %||% 0)
        bss_E_total <- bss_E_total + pe_all[[pe_label]]$effort_total
      } else {
        n <- length(b$C_draws)
        idx <- rep_len(1:n, n_draws_max)
        bss_C_total <- bss_C_total + b$C_draws[idx]
        bss_E_total <- bss_E_total + b$E_draws[idx]
      }
    }
  }
  bss_C_total <- bss_C_total + (pe_all$comm_charter[[cg]] %||% 0)
  bss_E_total <- bss_E_total + pe_all$comm_charter$effort_total

  final_table <- tibble(
    Catch_Group = cg,
    Estimate = c("Effort (crabber-hrs)", "Catch"),
    PE = c(round(pe_summary$Effort_hrs[6]),
           round(if(cg=="Dungeness_Kept") pe_summary$Dungeness[6] else pe_summary$Red_Rock[6])),
    BSS_median = c(round(median(bss_E_total)), round(median(bss_C_total))),
    BSS_lo95 = c(round(quantile(bss_E_total,0.025)), round(quantile(bss_C_total,0.025))),
    BSS_hi95 = c(round(quantile(bss_E_total,0.975)), round(quantile(bss_C_total,0.975)))
  )

  cat(sprintf("\n--- Port Total: %s ---\n", cg))
  print(final_table)
  write.csv(final_table, file.path(output_dir, paste0("port_total_",cg,".csv")), row.names=FALSE)
}
## 
## --- Port Total: Dungeness_Kept ---
## # A tibble: 2 × 6
##   Catch_Group    Estimate                 PE BSS_median BSS_lo95 BSS_hi95
##   <chr>          <chr>                 <dbl>      <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 Dungeness_Kept Effort (crabber-hrs) 176208     424895   370608   499909
## 2 Dungeness_Kept Catch                 74095     217971   163649   313836
# ===========================================================================
# 7.8 MONTHLY ESTIMATES WITH UNCERTAINTY
# v5.1: Uses C_total (vector[D]) instead of C[S][D,G]
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n MONTHLY ESTIMATES\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  MONTHLY ESTIMATES
##  ============================================================
days_full <- prep_days_crab(params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end,
                            params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                            params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)

month_lookup <- days_full |>
  mutate(month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m")) |>
  select(event_date, month_label)

all_months <- sort(unique(month_lookup$month_label))
monthly_C_draws <- matrix(0, nrow = n_draws_max, ncol = length(all_months),
                          dimnames = list(NULL, all_months))
monthly_E_draws <- matrix(0, nrow = n_draws_max, ncol = length(all_months),
                          dimnames = list(NULL, all_months))

for(cg in bss_catch_groups) {
  for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
    for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
      label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_", cg)
      pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
      b <- bss_all[[label]]

      days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                                params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
      days_ss$month_label <- format(days_ss$event_date, "%Y-%m")

      if(bss_use_pe_for(b)) {   # B1.8: PE if insufficient data OR failed the gate
        summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)
        pe_daily <- summ_ss$effort_index |>
          filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
          group_by(event_date) |>
          summarise(mean_count = mean(count_quantity), .groups = "drop") |>
          left_join(days_ss |> select(event_date, day_length, month_label), by = "event_date") |>
          mutate(daily_effort = mean_count * summ_ss$crabbers_per_gear * day_length)

        monthly_pe <- pe_daily |>
          group_by(month_label) |>
          summarise(month_effort = sum(daily_effort), .groups = "drop") |>
          mutate(share = month_effort / sum(month_effort))

        pe_catch_total <- pe_all[[pe_label]][[cg]] %||% 0
        pe_effort_total <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$effort_total

        for(m in monthly_pe$month_label) {
          if(m %in% all_months) {
            sh <- monthly_pe$share[monthly_pe$month_label == m]
            monthly_C_draws[, m] <- monthly_C_draws[, m] + pe_catch_total * sh
            monthly_E_draws[, m] <- monthly_E_draws[, m] + pe_effort_total * sh
          }
        }

      } else {
        # Use C_gear_pred (predictive, carries Poisson noise) for monthly
        # totals so predictive uncertainty propagates into monthly CIs.
        # C_total is now expected value (smooth) — good for daily plots but
        # would underestimate monthly CI widths.
        C_pred_mat <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "C_gear_pred")$C_gear_pred  # [draws, D, G_gear]
        # Sum across gear types to get daily total with full uncertainty
        C_daily_mat <- apply(C_pred_mat, c(1, 2), sum)  # [draws, D]
        E_bss <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "E")$E  # [draws, S, D, G]
        E_daily_mat <- E_bss[,1,,1]
        n_fit_draws <- nrow(C_daily_mat)

        for(m in unique(days_ss$month_label)) {
          day_idx <- which(days_ss$month_label == m)
          if(length(day_idx) == 0) next

          if(length(day_idx) == 1) {
            month_C <- C_daily_mat[, day_idx]
            month_E <- E_daily_mat[, day_idx]
          } else {
            month_C <- rowSums(C_daily_mat[, day_idx])
            month_E <- rowSums(E_daily_mat[, day_idx])
          }

          idx <- rep_len(1:n_fit_draws, n_draws_max)
          if(m %in% all_months) {
            monthly_C_draws[, m] <- monthly_C_draws[, m] + month_C[idx]
            monthly_E_draws[, m] <- monthly_E_draws[, m] + month_E[idx]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
# Add commercial/charter to census months
if(!is.null(pe_all$comm_charter$daily_est)) {
  comm_daily <- pe_all$comm_charter$daily_est |>
    mutate(month_label = format(date, "%Y-%m"))

  comm_total <- pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept
  total_sampled_dung <- sum(comm_daily$est_dung)
  if(total_sampled_dung > 0) {
    for(m in unique(comm_daily$month_label)) {
      if(m %in% all_months) {
        share <- sum(comm_daily$est_dung[comm_daily$month_label == m]) / total_sampled_dung
        monthly_C_draws[, m] <- monthly_C_draws[, m] + comm_total * share
      }
    }
  }
}

monthly_summary <- tibble(
  Month = all_months,
  BSS_Catch_median = apply(monthly_C_draws, 2, median),
  BSS_Catch_lo95   = apply(monthly_C_draws, 2, quantile, 0.025),
  BSS_Catch_hi95   = apply(monthly_C_draws, 2, quantile, 0.975),
  BSS_Effort_median = apply(monthly_E_draws, 2, median),
  BSS_Effort_lo95   = apply(monthly_E_draws, 2, quantile, 0.025),
  BSS_Effort_hi95   = apply(monthly_E_draws, 2, quantile, 0.975)
) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round))

cat("\n--- Monthly Dungeness Catch Estimates (all populations combined) ---\n")
## 
## --- Monthly Dungeness Catch Estimates (all populations combined) ---
print(monthly_summary |> select(Month, BSS_Catch_median, BSS_Catch_lo95, BSS_Catch_hi95))
## # A tibble: 13 × 4
##    Month   BSS_Catch_median BSS_Catch_lo95 BSS_Catch_hi95
##    <chr>              <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl>
##  1 2024-09             2989           2989           2989
##  2 2024-10             6305           6305           6305
##  3 2024-11             5054           5054           5054
##  4 2024-12            15555          13562          19377
##  5 2025-01            12843          11194          16265
##  6 2025-02             4020           3335           5793
##  7 2025-03             5861           4301           9963
##  8 2025-04            12328           8142          21623
##  9 2025-05            19801          12002          36389
## 10 2025-06             8342           3444          19966
## 11 2025-07            41839          18974          93038
## 12 2025-08            54985          31588         101841
## 13 2025-09            21807          10389          46801
write.csv(monthly_summary, file.path(output_dir, "monthly_estimates.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Monthly catch plot
p_monthly <- monthly_summary |>
  mutate(Month = factor(Month, levels = all_months)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Month)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = BSS_Catch_median), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = BSS_Catch_lo95, ymax = BSS_Catch_hi95), width = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = which(all_months == format(as.Date(params$pot_open_date), "%Y-%m")) - 0.5,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Monthly Dungeness Catch Estimate (BSS + PE combined)",
       subtitle = "Bars = median, whiskers = 95% credible interval",
       x = "Month", y = "Dungeness crab caught") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_monthly_catch.png"), p_monthly, width = 12, height = 6)
print(p_monthly)

# Monthly effort plot
p_monthly_eff <- monthly_summary |>
  mutate(Month = factor(Month, levels = all_months)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Month)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = BSS_Effort_median), fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = BSS_Effort_lo95, ymax = BSS_Effort_hi95), width = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = which(all_months == format(as.Date(params$pot_open_date), "%Y-%m")) - 0.5,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Monthly Effort Estimate (BSS + PE combined)",
       subtitle = "Bars = median, whiskers = 95% credible interval",
       x = "Month", y = "Crabber-hours") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_monthly_effort.png"), p_monthly_eff, width = 12, height = 6)
print(p_monthly_eff)

# ===========================================================================
# 7.9 CATCH BY CRABBING MODE
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CATCH BY CRABBING MODE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  CATCH BY CRABBING MODE
##  ============================================================
mode_table <- tibble(
  Mode = c("Shore (Dock/Jetty/Beach)", "Private Boat", "Commercial/Charter", "PORT TOTAL"),
  PE_Catch = c(
    (pe_all$shore_ring_net_only$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0) +
      (pe_all$shore_all_gear$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0),
    (pe_all$private_boat_ring_net_only$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0) +
      (pe_all$private_boat_all_gear$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0),
    pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0,
    NA
  ),
  PE_Effort = c(
    (pe_all$shore_ring_net_only$effort_total %||% 0) +
      (pe_all$shore_all_gear$effort_total %||% 0),
    (pe_all$private_boat_ring_net_only$effort_total %||% 0) +
      (pe_all$private_boat_all_gear$effort_total %||% 0),
    pe_all$comm_charter$effort_total %||% 0,
    NA
  )
)
mode_table$PE_Catch[4] <- sum(mode_table$PE_Catch[1:3], na.rm = TRUE)
mode_table$PE_Effort[4] <- sum(mode_table$PE_Effort[1:3], na.rm = TRUE)

print(mode_table |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round)))
## # A tibble: 4 × 3
##   Mode                     PE_Catch PE_Effort
##   <chr>                       <dbl>     <dbl>
## 1 Shore (Dock/Jetty/Beach)    49981    139570
## 2 Private Boat                12107     36355
## 3 Commercial/Charter          12007       283
## 4 PORT TOTAL                  74095    176208
write.csv(mode_table, file.path(output_dir, "catch_by_mode.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.10 BSS CATCH BY GEAR TYPE
# v5.1: PE fallback uses word-boundary regex consistent with BSS
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n BSS CATCH BY GEAR TYPE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  BSS CATCH BY GEAR TYPE
##  ============================================================
gear_results <- list()

for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label_cg <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_Dungeness_Kept")
    pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    b <- bss_all[[label_cg]]

    if(is.null(b)) next

    if(bss_use_pe_for(b)) {   # B1.8: PE if insufficient data OR failed the gate
      pe_catch_total <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0
      # v5.1: Word-boundary regex consistent with BSS classification
      int_ss <- dwg$interview |>
        filter(population == pop,
               between(event_date, ss$start, ss$end),
               dungeness_kept > 0) |>
        mutate(
          has_pot      = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bpot\\b") &
                                    !str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bslip\\s*ring\\b")),
          has_ring_net = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bring\\s*net\\b")),
          has_trap     = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\b(trap|star)\\b")),
          has_snare    = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bsnare\\b"))
        )
      # Apply regulatory exclusion consistent with BSS
      gear_exclude_ss <- ss$gear_exclude %||% character(0)
      if(length(gear_exclude_ss) > 0) {
        excl_cols <- c(Pot="has_pot", `Ring Net`="has_ring_net",
                       Trap="has_trap", Snare="has_snare")
        for(gt in gear_exclude_ss) {
          if(gt %in% names(excl_cols)) int_ss[[excl_cols[gt]]] <- 0L
        }
      }
      int_ss <- int_ss |>
        mutate(n_types = pmax(has_pot + has_ring_net + has_trap + has_snare, 1L))
      # Weighted allocation of catch across gear types
      gear_catch_alloc <- c(
        Pot = sum(int_ss$dungeness_kept * int_ss$has_pot / int_ss$n_types),
        `Ring Net` = sum(int_ss$dungeness_kept * int_ss$has_ring_net / int_ss$n_types),
        Trap = sum(int_ss$dungeness_kept * int_ss$has_trap / int_ss$n_types),
        Snare = sum(int_ss$dungeness_kept * int_ss$has_snare / int_ss$n_types)
      )
      gear_catch_alloc <- gear_catch_alloc[gear_catch_alloc > 0]
      total_alloc <- sum(gear_catch_alloc)
      if(total_alloc > 0) {
        props <- gear_catch_alloc / total_alloc
      } else {
        props <- c(Other = 1.0)
      }
      for(gt in names(props)) {
        gear_results[[length(gear_results)+1]] <- tibble(
          population=pop, subseason=ss$name, gear_type=gt,
          BSS_median=NA, BSS_lo95=NA, BSS_hi95=NA,
          PE_catch=round(pe_catch_total * props[gt]),
          method="PE (weighted proportional)"
        )
      }
    } else {
      labels <- b$gear_type_labels
      draws <- b$C_gear_draws
      for(gg in seq_along(labels)) {
        g_draws <- if(is.matrix(draws)) draws[,gg] else draws
        gear_results[[length(gear_results)+1]] <- tibble(
          population=pop, subseason=ss$name, gear_type=labels[gg],
          BSS_median=round(median(g_draws)),
          BSS_lo95=round(quantile(g_draws, 0.025)),
          BSS_hi95=round(quantile(g_draws, 0.975)),
          PE_catch=NA, method="BSS"
        )
      }
    }
  }
}

# Add commercial/charter as Pot
comm_catch <- pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0
gear_results[[length(gear_results)+1]] <- tibble(
  population="comm_charter", subseason="census", gear_type="Pot",
  BSS_median=NA, BSS_lo95=NA, BSS_hi95=NA,
  PE_catch=round(comm_catch), method="Census"
)

gear_df <- bind_rows(gear_results)
cat("\n--- Catch by Gear Type (BSS where available, PE fallback) ---\n")
## 
## --- Catch by Gear Type (BSS where available, PE fallback) ---
print(gear_df)
## # A tibble: 12 × 8
##    population   subseason     gear_type BSS_median BSS_lo95 BSS_hi95 PE_catch method                    
##    <chr>        <chr>         <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>                     
##  1 shore        ring_net_only Ring Net          NA       NA       NA     4844 PE (weighted proportional)
##  2 shore        ring_net_only Trap              NA       NA       NA     7137 PE (weighted proportional)
##  3 shore        ring_net_only Snare             NA       NA       NA     1422 PE (weighted proportional)
##  4 shore        all_gear      Pot               NA       NA       NA     9665 PE (weighted proportional)
##  5 shore        all_gear      Ring Net          NA       NA       NA     6515 PE (weighted proportional)
##  6 shore        all_gear      Trap              NA       NA       NA    15995 PE (weighted proportional)
##  7 shore        all_gear      Snare             NA       NA       NA     4402 PE (weighted proportional)
##  8 private_boat ring_net_only Ring Net          NA       NA       NA      829 PE (weighted proportional)
##  9 private_boat ring_net_only Trap              NA       NA       NA       58 PE (weighted proportional)
## 10 private_boat ring_net_only Snare             NA       NA       NA       58 PE (weighted proportional)
## 11 private_boat all_gear      All           155038   100717   250903       NA BSS                       
## 12 comm_charter census        Pot               NA       NA       NA    12007 Census
write.csv(gear_df, file.path(output_dir, "catch_by_gear_type_detail.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Port-level summary by gear type
gear_port <- gear_df |>
  mutate(best_catch = coalesce(BSS_median, PE_catch)) |>
  group_by(gear_type) |>
  summarise(
    catch = sum(best_catch, na.rm=TRUE),
    has_BSS = any(method == "BSS"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(catch)) |>
  mutate(proportion = round(catch / sum(catch), 3))

cat("\n--- Port Total by Gear Type ---\n")
## 
## --- Port Total by Gear Type ---
print(gear_port)
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   gear_type  catch has_BSS proportion
##   <chr>      <dbl> <lgl>        <dbl>
## 1 All       155038 TRUE         0.711
## 2 Trap       23190 FALSE        0.106
## 3 Pot        21672 FALSE        0.099
## 4 Ring Net   12188 FALSE        0.056
## 5 Snare       5882 FALSE        0.027
write.csv(gear_port, file.path(output_dir, "catch_by_gear_type.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Gear type bar chart
p_gear <- gear_port |>
  mutate(gear_type = fct_reorder(gear_type, catch)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gear_type, y = catch, fill = gear_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%s\n(%.0f%%)", format(catch, big.mark=","), proportion*100)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Estimated Dungeness Catch by Gear Type",
       subtitle = "From BSS gear-type CPUE model (v5.2)",
       x = "Gear Type", y = "Estimated Catch") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_flip()
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "plot_catch_by_gear_type.png"), p_gear, width = 10, height = 6)
print(p_gear)

cat("\n  Gear-type outputs saved.\n")
## 
##   Gear-type outputs saved.
# ===========================================================================
# 7.11 CONVERGENCE REPORT
#
# v5.1: Extended diagnostics — checks C_sum_gear, r_C_gear, sigma_eps_C_gear
#        in addition to C_sum and E_sum (Issue 11)
# v5.2: Divergent transitions now included in pass/fail criterion.
#        Treedepth exceedance warnings added.
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n CONVERGENCE REPORT\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  CONVERGENCE REPORT
##  ============================================================
convergence_report <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(!is.null(b$pe_fallback) && b$pe_fallback) {
    convergence_report[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label, divergences = NA, treedepth_pct = NA,
      C_sum_rhat = NA, E_sum_rhat = NA, C_sum_neff = NA, E_sum_neff = NA,
      max_gear_rhat = NA, min_gear_neff = NA,
      max_r_C_rhat = NA, max_sigma_C_rhat = NA,
      pass_convergence = FALSE, method_selected = "PE (insufficient data)"
    )
  } else {
    sp <- rstan::get_sampler_params(b$fit, inc_warmup = FALSE)
    n_divergent <- sum(sapply(sp, function(x) sum(x[,"divergent__"])))
    n_treedepth <- sum(sapply(sp, function(x) sum(x[,"treedepth__"] >=
      b$fit@stan_args[[1]]$control$max_treedepth)))
    n_total <- sum(sapply(sp, nrow))

    # Core diagnostics: C_sum and E_sum
    summ_core <- summary(b$fit, pars = c("C_sum","E_sum"))$summary
    rhat_C <- summ_core["C_sum","Rhat"]; rhat_E <- summ_core["E_sum","Rhat"]
    neff_C <- summ_core["C_sum","n_eff"]; neff_E <- summ_core["E_sum","n_eff"]

    # v5.1 Extended: Per-gear-type diagnostics
    summ_gear <- summary(b$fit, pars = "C_sum_gear")$summary
    summ_r_C <- summary(b$fit, pars = "r_C_gear_out")$summary
    summ_sig_C <- summary(b$fit, pars = "sigma_eps_C_gear_out")$summary

    max_gear_rhat <- max(summ_gear[,"Rhat"], na.rm=TRUE)
    min_gear_neff <- min(summ_gear[,"n_eff"], na.rm=TRUE)
    max_r_C_rhat <- max(summ_r_C[,"Rhat"], na.rm=TRUE)
    max_sigma_C_rhat <- max(summ_sig_C[,"Rhat"], na.rm=TRUE)

    # Convergence criteria: ALL monitored parameters must pass
    all_rhats <- c(rhat_C, rhat_E, summ_gear[,"Rhat"],
                   summ_r_C[,"Rhat"], summ_sig_C[,"Rhat"])
    all_neffs <- c(neff_C, neff_E, summ_gear[,"n_eff"])

    pass_rhat <- max(all_rhats, na.rm=TRUE) < 1.01  # v5.4: tightened from 1.05 (Vehtari et al. 2021)
    pass_neff <- min(all_neffs, na.rm=TRUE) > 400
    # B1.8: the PE/BSS decision is the scale-aware gate (bss_compute_gate),
    # already computed and stored on the fit. Use it here so the report's
    # pass_convergence / method_selected match what actually selected PE vs BSS.
    # The gear-type diagnostics above are retained for insight; the divergence
    # fraction and impact values that drove the decision are printed to the
    # console by the gate and stored in b$gate_info.
    gi <- b$gate_info
    pass <- isTRUE(gi$pass_convergence)

    # v5.2: Warn on treedepth exceedances
    if(n_treedepth / n_total > 0.05) {
      cat(sprintf("  WARNING: %s — %.1f%% treedepth exceedances (consider increasing max_treedepth)\n",
                  label, n_treedepth / n_total * 100))
    }
    # v5.2: Warn on divergences even if within threshold
    if(n_divergent > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  WARNING: %s — %d divergent transitions detected\n", label, n_divergent))
    }

    convergence_report[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      divergences = n_divergent,
      treedepth_pct = round(n_treedepth / n_total * 100, 1),
      C_sum_rhat = round(rhat_C, 4),
      E_sum_rhat = round(rhat_E, 4),
      C_sum_neff = round(neff_C),
      E_sum_neff = round(neff_E),
      max_gear_rhat = round(max_gear_rhat, 4),
      min_gear_neff = round(min_gear_neff),
      max_r_C_rhat = round(max_r_C_rhat, 4),
      max_sigma_C_rhat = round(max_sigma_C_rhat, 4),
      pass_convergence = pass,
      method_selected = if(pass) "BSS" else "PE (convergence fail)"
    )

    # Warn if gear-type parameters failed but aggregates passed
    if(!pass && max(c(rhat_C, rhat_E), na.rm=TRUE) < 1.01) {
      cat(sprintf("  WARNING: %s — C_sum/E_sum converged but gear-type parameters did not.\n", label))
      cat(sprintf("    max gear Rhat=%.3f, max r_C Rhat=%.3f, max sigma_C Rhat=%.3f\n",
                  max_gear_rhat, max_r_C_rhat, max_sigma_C_rhat))
    }
  }
}
##   WARNING: shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept — 7818 divergent transitions detected
##   WARNING: shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept — 7807 divergent transitions detected
##   WARNING: shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept — C_sum/E_sum converged but gear-type parameters did not.
##     max gear Rhat=1.001, max r_C Rhat=1.006, max sigma_C Rhat=1.010
##   WARNING: private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept — 109 divergent transitions detected
convergence_df <- bind_rows(convergence_report)
cat("\n")
print(convergence_df)
## # A tibble: 4 × 13
##   fit                  divergences treedepth_pct C_sum_rhat E_sum_rhat C_sum_neff E_sum_neff max_gear_rhat min_gear_neff max_r_C_rhat max_sigma_C_rhat
##   <chr>                      <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>            <dbl>
## 1 shore_ring_net_only…        7818             0       1.06      1.02          61        969          1.06            61        1.05              1.06
## 2 shore_all_gear_Dung…        7807             0       1.00      1.00        1280        368          1.00          1280        1.01              1.01
## 3 private_boat_ring_n…          NA            NA      NA        NA             NA         NA         NA               NA       NA                NA   
## 4 private_boat_all_ge…         109             0       1.00      1.000       2037       4031          1.00          2037        1.000             1.00
## # ℹ 2 more variables: pass_convergence <lgl>, method_selected <chr>
write.csv(convergence_df, file.path(output_dir, "convergence_report.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.11b EXTENDED DIAGNOSTICS + PSIS-LOO
# Shared writers auto-sourced from 03_R_functions/:
#   write_bss_diagnostics()            structural params, divergence localization,
#                                      PPC calibration + PIT histogram
#   write_effort_overdispersion_diag()  Poisson / NB / latent variance decomposition
#   write_fit_extended_diagnostics()    O1-O9 (E-BFMI, AR paths, per-obs PIT,
#                                      prior-vs-posterior contraction, ...)
#   write_loo_diagnostics()             O13 PSIS-LOO + Pareto-k, from the
#                                      log_lik_gear/trailer/catch added to the
#                                      gear-resolved Stan model.
#
# These run for every fit that PRODUCED draws, including fits the convergence
# gate rejected. That is deliberate: a gate-failed fit reports PE in the totals,
# but its diagnostics are exactly what identify WHY it failed.
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EXTENDED DIAGNOSTICS\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  EXTENDED DIAGNOSTICS
##  ============================================================
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$fit)) next   # PE-only (insufficient data): no draws to diagnose

  cat(sprintf("\n--- diagnostics: %s (%s) ---\n", label,
              if(isTRUE(b$use_bss)) "gate PASS" else "gate FAIL -> reporting PE"))

  write_bss_diagnostics(b$fit, b$bss_data, label, output_dir)
  write_effort_overdispersion_diag(b$fit, b$bss_data, label, output_dir)
  write_fit_extended_diagnostics(b$fit, b$bss_data, b$days_ss, label, output_dir)
  write_loo_diagnostics(b$fit, b$bss_data, b$days_ss, label, output_dir)
}
## 
## --- diagnostics: shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept (gate FAIL -> reporting PE) ---
##     [diag] shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept: 7818/8000 divergent (97.7%); distortion C/E = 3.3%/2.3%
##     [diag] PPC coverage (nominal 50/95):
##       gear     50%=58%  95%=98%  (n=52)
##       catch    50%=65%  95%=98%  (n=627)
##   shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept [gear]: n=52 | shares pois/NB/latent = 0.06/0.82/0.11 | pred VMR 14.37 (NB-only 12.96) | PPC cov50 0.58, pit_sd 0.262
##       -> observation NB over-dispersion dominates: lever is r_E / sigma_r_E prior (T1.5 5a; pair with T1.3)
## 
## --- diagnostics: shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept (gate FAIL -> reporting PE) ---
##     [diag] shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 7807/8000 divergent (97.6%); distortion C/E = 0.4%/0.0%
##     [diag] PPC coverage (nominal 50/95):
##       gear     50%=56%  95%=95%  (n=311)
##       catch    50%=74%  95%=98%  (n=1651)
##   shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept [gear]: n=311 | shares pois/NB/latent = 0.05/0.91/0.04 | pred VMR 15.80 (NB-only 15.23) | PPC cov50 0.56, pit_sd 0.278
##       -> observation NB over-dispersion dominates: lever is r_E / sigma_r_E prior (T1.5 5a; pair with T1.3)
## 
## --- diagnostics: private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept (gate PASS) ---
##     [diag] private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept: 109/4000 divergent (2.7%); distortion C/E = 4.2%/1.5%
##     [diag] PPC coverage (nominal 50/95):
##       trailer  50%=68%  95%=98%  (n=195)
##       catch    50%=51%  95%=98%  (n=131)
##   private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept [trailer]: n=195 | shares pois/NB/latent = 0.03/0.90/0.07 | pred VMR 13.63 (NB-only 12.81) | PPC cov50 0.68, pit_sd 0.268
##       -> observation NB over-dispersion dominates: lever is r_E / sigma_r_E prior (T1.5 5a; pair with T1.3)
## Warning: Some Pareto k diagnostic values are too high. See help('pareto-k-diagnostic') for details.
# Run-level rollups (O10-O12). gear_props = NULL: the gear-resolved model carries
# gear composition via PE apportionment, not as a run-level Stan object, so O10
# is skipped for this track.
write_run_level_diagnostics(bss_all, pe_all, gear_props = NULL,
                            params = params, output_dir = output_dir)
##     [save:O7] skipped: no parameter C_expected
# ===========================================================================
# 7.12 DAILY COMBINED ESTIMATE
# v5.1: Uses C_total instead of C[S][D,G]
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n DAILY COMBINED ESTIMATE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  DAILY COMBINED ESTIMATE
##  ============================================================
daily_combined <- list()

for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label_cg <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_Dungeness_Kept")
    pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    b <- bss_all[[label_cg]]

    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                              params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

    pe_daily <- summ_ss$effort_index |>
      filter(count_sequence <= params$bss_max_count_seq) |>
      group_by(event_date) |>
      summarise(mean_count = mean(count_quantity), .groups = "drop") |>
      mutate(pe_effort = mean_count * summ_ss$crabbers_per_gear) |>
      left_join(days_ss |> select(event_date, day_type, day_length), by = "event_date") |>
      mutate(pe_crabber_hours = pe_effort * day_length)

    # v5.2: Apply incomplete trip filter to PE CPUE in daily combined
    pe_cpue_int <- summ_ss$interview |> filter(fishing_time_total > 0)
    if(isTRUE(params$filter_incomplete_trips)) {
      pe_cpue_int <- pe_cpue_int |> filter(trip_status == "Complete" | is.na(trip_status))
    }
    pe_cpue <- pe_cpue_int |>
      group_by(event_date) |>
      summarise(catch = sum(Dungeness_Kept, na.rm=TRUE),
                hrs = sum(fishing_time_total, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop") |>
      mutate(pe_cpue = if_else(hrs > 0, catch/hrs, NA_real_))

    daily_pop <- days_ss |>
      select(event_date, day_type, day_length) |>
      mutate(population = pop, subseason = ss$name) |>
      left_join(pe_daily |> select(event_date, pe_crabber_hours), by = "event_date") |>
      left_join(pe_cpue |> select(event_date, pe_cpue), by = "event_date") |>
      mutate(pe_catch = pe_crabber_hours * pe_cpue)

    if(!is.null(b) && !bss_use_pe_for(b)) {   # B1.8: BSS only if it passed the gate
      E_bss <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "E")$E
      # v5.2: C_total is now expected value (smooth, no Poisson noise).
      # Good for daily trajectory plots. Season totals use C_gear_pred.
      C_total_bss <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "C_total")$C_total  # [draws, D]

      daily_pop$bss_effort_median <- apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, median)
      daily_pop$bss_effort_lo95  <- apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, quantile, 0.025)
      daily_pop$bss_effort_hi95  <- apply(E_bss[,1,,1], 2, quantile, 0.975)
      daily_pop$bss_catch_median <- apply(C_total_bss, 2, median)
      daily_pop$bss_catch_lo95   <- apply(C_total_bss, 2, quantile, 0.025)
      daily_pop$bss_catch_hi95   <- apply(C_total_bss, 2, quantile, 0.975)

      conv <- convergence_df |> filter(fit == label_cg)
      use_bss <- nrow(conv) > 0 && conv$pass_convergence[1]

      daily_pop$combined_effort <- if(use_bss) daily_pop$bss_effort_median else daily_pop$pe_crabber_hours
      daily_pop$combined_catch  <- if(use_bss) daily_pop$bss_catch_median  else daily_pop$pe_catch
      daily_pop$method <- if(use_bss) "BSS" else "PE"
    } else {
      daily_pop$bss_effort_median <- NA; daily_pop$bss_effort_lo95 <- NA; daily_pop$bss_effort_hi95 <- NA
      daily_pop$bss_catch_median <- NA; daily_pop$bss_catch_lo95 <- NA; daily_pop$bss_catch_hi95 <- NA
      daily_pop$combined_effort <- daily_pop$pe_crabber_hours
      daily_pop$combined_catch  <- daily_pop$pe_catch
      daily_pop$method <- "PE"
    }

    daily_combined[[length(daily_combined)+1]] <- daily_pop
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
daily_combined_df <- bind_rows(daily_combined) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 1))) |>
  arrange(event_date, population)

cat(sprintf("  Daily combined estimate: %d rows (%d days × %d populations)\n",
            nrow(daily_combined_df), n_distinct(daily_combined_df$event_date),
            n_distinct(daily_combined_df$population)))
##   Daily combined estimate: 730 rows (365 days × 2 populations)
write.csv(daily_combined_df, file.path(output_dir, "daily_combined_estimate.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.13 DATA COVERAGE TABLE
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n DATA COVERAGE\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  DATA COVERAGE
##  ============================================================
coverage <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                              params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

    days_ss$month_label <- format(days_ss$event_date, "%Y-%m")

    total_days <- days_ss |> count(month_label, name = "total_days")

    effort_days <- summ_ss$effort_index |>
      mutate(month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m")) |>
      distinct(event_date, month_label) |>
      count(month_label, name = "effort_days")

    int_days <- summ_ss$interview |>
      mutate(month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m")) |>
      distinct(event_date, month_label) |>
      count(month_label, name = "interview_days")

    int_count <- summ_ss$interview |>
      mutate(month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m")) |>
      count(month_label, name = "n_interviews")

    cov <- total_days |>
      left_join(effort_days, by = "month_label") |>
      left_join(int_days, by = "month_label") |>
      left_join(int_count, by = "month_label") |>
      mutate(across(c(effort_days, interview_days, n_interviews), ~replace_na(., 0)),
             effort_coverage_pct = round(effort_days / total_days * 100, 0),
             population = pop, subseason = ss$name)

    coverage[[length(coverage)+1]] <- cov
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
coverage_df <- bind_rows(coverage) |>
  select(population, subseason, month_label, total_days, effort_days, effort_coverage_pct,
         interview_days, n_interviews) |>
  arrange(population, month_label)

cat("\n")
print(coverage_df, n = 30)
## # A tibble: 26 × 8
##    population   subseason     month_label total_days effort_days effort_coverage_pct interview_days n_interviews
##    <chr>        <chr>         <chr>            <int>       <int>               <dbl>          <int>        <int>
##  1 private_boat ring_net_only 2024-09             15           7                  47              3            3
##  2 private_boat ring_net_only 2024-10             31          21                  68              8           11
##  3 private_boat ring_net_only 2024-11             30          15                  50              2            3
##  4 private_boat all_gear      2024-12             31          22                  71             17           40
##  5 private_boat all_gear      2025-01             31          21                  68             15           35
##  6 private_boat all_gear      2025-02             28          17                  61              4            5
##  7 private_boat all_gear      2025-03             31          18                  58              2            6
##  8 private_boat all_gear      2025-04             30          14                  47              7           11
##  9 private_boat all_gear      2025-05             31          14                  45              7           11
## 10 private_boat all_gear      2025-06             30           6                  20              3            6
## 11 private_boat all_gear      2025-07             31           9                  29              4            8
## 12 private_boat all_gear      2025-08             31          14                  45              7           16
## 13 private_boat all_gear      2025-09             15           8                  53              5            7
## 14 shore        ring_net_only 2024-09             15           9                  60              9          146
## 15 shore        ring_net_only 2024-10             31          23                  74             23          343
## 16 shore        ring_net_only 2024-11             30          20                  67             19          367
## 17 shore        all_gear      2024-12             31          22                  71             23          373
## 18 shore        all_gear      2025-01             31          21                  68             21          310
## 19 shore        all_gear      2025-02             28          17                  61             17          186
## 20 shore        all_gear      2025-03             31          19                  61             19          247
## 21 shore        all_gear      2025-04             30          14                  47             14          347
## 22 shore        all_gear      2025-05             31          14                  45             14          284
## 23 shore        all_gear      2025-06             30           6                  20              6          138
## 24 shore        all_gear      2025-07             31           9                  29              9          220
## 25 shore        all_gear      2025-08             31          14                  45             14          419
## 26 shore        all_gear      2025-09             15           9                  60              9          219
write.csv(coverage_df, file.path(output_dir, "data_coverage.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.14 EFFORT MULTIPLIER SUMMARY (B1, B2)
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EFFORT MULTIPLIERS (B1, B2)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  EFFORT MULTIPLIERS (B1, B2)
##  ============================================================
multiplier_results <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    B1_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B1")$B1
    B2_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "B2")$B2

    weekend_mult <- exp(B1_draws)
    holiday_mult <- exp(B1_draws + B2_draws)

    multiplier_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      B1_median = round(median(B1_draws), 3),
      B1_lo95 = round(quantile(B1_draws, 0.025), 3),
      B1_hi95 = round(quantile(B1_draws, 0.975), 3),
      weekend_multiplier = sprintf("%.1fx (%.1f-%.1f)",
        median(weekend_mult), quantile(weekend_mult, 0.025), quantile(weekend_mult, 0.975)),
      B2_median = round(median(B2_draws), 3),
      B2_lo95 = round(quantile(B2_draws, 0.025), 3),
      B2_hi95 = round(quantile(B2_draws, 0.975), 3),
      holiday_multiplier = sprintf("%.1fx (%.1f-%.1f)",
        median(holiday_mult), quantile(holiday_mult, 0.025), quantile(holiday_mult, 0.975))
    )
  }
}
if(length(multiplier_results) > 0) {
  multiplier_df <- bind_rows(multiplier_results)
  cat("\n  Effort multipliers relative to weekdays:\n")
  for(i in 1:nrow(multiplier_df)) {
    cat(sprintf("    %s:\n      Weekends: %s\n      Holidays: %s\n",
                multiplier_df$fit[i],
                multiplier_df$weekend_multiplier[i],
                multiplier_df$holiday_multiplier[i]))
  }
  write.csv(multiplier_df, file.path(output_dir, "effort_multipliers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}
## 
##   Effort multipliers relative to weekdays:
##     shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept:
##       Weekends: 1.7x (1.3-2.2)
##       Holidays: 1.9x (0.7-5.0)
##     shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept:
##       Weekends: 2.2x (1.9-2.4)
##       Holidays: 3.2x (2.2-4.6)
##     private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept:
##       Weekends: 1.3x (1.0-1.8)
##       Holidays: 2.2x (1.1-5.0)
# ===========================================================================
# 7.15 INTERVIEW CPUE SUMMARY BY MONTH × POPULATION
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n INTERVIEW CPUE SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  INTERVIEW CPUE SUMMARY
##  ============================================================
cpue_summary <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(fishing_time_total > 0, population %in% c("shore","private_boat")) |>
  mutate(
    month_label = format(event_date, "%Y-%m"),
    cpue = dungeness_kept / fishing_time_total
  ) |>
  group_by(population, month_label) |>
  summarise(
    n_interviews = n(),
    mean_cpue = round(mean(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    sd_cpue = round(sd(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    se_cpue = round(sd(cpue, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n()), 3),
    median_cpue = round(median(cpue, na.rm=TRUE), 3),
    mean_dungeness = round(mean(dungeness_kept, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    mean_hours = round(mean(fishing_time_total, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(population, month_label)

cat("\n")
print(cpue_summary, n = 30)
## # A tibble: 26 × 9
##    population   month_label n_interviews mean_cpue sd_cpue se_cpue median_cpue mean_dungeness mean_hours
##    <chr>        <chr>              <int>     <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 private_boat 2024-09                3     0.333   0.577   0.333       0                2          6  
##  2 private_boat 2024-10               11     1.22    0.99    0.299       1.07             6.2        5.9
##  3 private_boat 2024-11                3     3.67    2.08    1.20        3               12          5  
##  4 private_boat 2024-12               40     1.44    1.73    0.274       0.5             16.2       44.6
##  5 private_boat 2025-01               35     1.54    1.45    0.245       1.2             14.6       60.9
##  6 private_boat 2025-02                5     0.787   1.30    0.582       0                4         13.5
##  7 private_boat 2025-03                6     0.432   0.592   0.242       0.171            4.8        9.7
##  8 private_boat 2025-04               11     1.44    1.30    0.391       1                5          6.6
##  9 private_boat 2025-05               11     0.586   0.571   0.172       0.556           11.7       91.4
## 10 private_boat 2025-06                6     0.464   0.765   0.312       0.182            5.3       44.2
## 11 private_boat 2025-07                8     0.519   0.665   0.235       0.208            5.5       70.1
## 12 private_boat 2025-08               16     0.635   0.766   0.192       0.227            7.9       42.9
## 13 private_boat 2025-09                7     0.306   0.333   0.126       0.2              3.1       19.1
## 14 shore        2024-09              146     0.682   1.75    0.145       0.366            3.4        7.8
## 15 shore        2024-10              343     0.584   0.704   0.038       0.417            3.5        7.3
## 16 shore        2024-11              367     0.647   0.738   0.039       0.462            3.4        6.2
## 17 shore        2024-12              373     0.579   0.758   0.039       0.333            3.1        6.6
## 18 shore        2025-01              310     0.244   0.398   0.023       0                1.6        7.2
## 19 shore        2025-02              186     0.269   0.502   0.037       0                1.3        7.2
## 20 shore        2025-03              247     0.293   0.538   0.034       0.1              1.5        6.9
## 21 shore        2025-04              347     0.284   0.446   0.024       0.126            2.3       11.5
## 22 shore        2025-05              284     0.433   0.625   0.037       0.212            2.5        8.2
## 23 shore        2025-06              138     0.361   0.474   0.04        0.167            2.7       10.1
## 24 shore        2025-07              220     0.292   0.433   0.029       0.08             2.2        9.1
## 25 shore        2025-08              419     0.353   0.54    0.026       0.1              2.3        8.3
## 26 shore        2025-09              219     0.464   0.634   0.043       0.2              3.1        8.3
write.csv(cpue_summary, file.path(output_dir, "interview_cpue_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.16 R_G AND R_G_boat POSTERIOR SUMMARIES
# v5.3: Stan model now exposes R_G_boat (gear per boat group) in place of
#       R_T (trailers per group). For shore fits R_G_boat is sampled from
#       its prior but has no data attached, so it is reported only for
#       boat fits. For boat fits R_G is fixed at params$R_G_fixed_boat.
# v5.1: Notes R_G behavior for boat vs shore fits
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n EXPANSION RATIO POSTERIORS (R_G, R_G_boat)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  EXPANSION RATIO POSTERIORS (R_G, R_G_boat)
##  ============================================================
expansion_results <- list()
for(label in names(bss_all)) {
  b <- bss_all[[label]]
  if(is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback) {
    R_G_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G")$R_G

    # v5.3: R_G_boat is only data-informed for boat fits; report it there.
    is_shore_fit <- grepl("^shore_", label)
    R_G_status <- if(is_shore_fit) "estimated" else sprintf("fixed at %.2f", params$R_G_fixed_boat)

    if(!is_shore_fit) {
      R_G_boat_draws <- rstan::extract(b$fit, "R_G_boat")$R_G_boat
      R_G_boat_median <- round(median(R_G_boat_draws), 3)
      R_G_boat_lo95   <- round(quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.025), 3)
      R_G_boat_hi95   <- round(quantile(R_G_boat_draws, 0.975), 3)
      R_G_boat_interp <- sprintf("%.2f gear per boat group (%.2f-%.2f)",
        R_G_boat_median, R_G_boat_lo95, R_G_boat_hi95)
    } else {
      R_G_boat_median <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_lo95   <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_hi95   <- NA_real_
      R_G_boat_interp <- "not applicable (shore fit)"
    }

    expansion_results[[label]] <- tibble(
      fit = label,
      R_G_median = round(median(R_G_draws), 3),
      R_G_lo95 = round(quantile(R_G_draws, 0.025), 3),
      R_G_hi95 = round(quantile(R_G_draws, 0.975), 3),
      R_G_status = R_G_status,
      R_G_interpretation = sprintf("%.2f gear per crabber (%.2f-%.2f) [%s]",
        median(R_G_draws), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.025), quantile(R_G_draws, 0.975), R_G_status),
      R_G_boat_median = R_G_boat_median,
      R_G_boat_lo95 = R_G_boat_lo95,
      R_G_boat_hi95 = R_G_boat_hi95,
      R_G_boat_interpretation = R_G_boat_interp
    )
  }
}
if(length(expansion_results) > 0) {
  expansion_df <- bind_rows(expansion_results)
  cat("\n")
  for(i in 1:nrow(expansion_df)) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s:\n    R_G:      %s\n    R_G_boat: %s\n",
                expansion_df$fit[i],
                expansion_df$R_G_interpretation[i],
                expansion_df$R_G_boat_interpretation[i]))
  }
  write.csv(expansion_df, file.path(output_dir, "expansion_ratios.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}
## 
##   shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept:
##     R_G:      1.32 gear per crabber (1.27-1.39) [estimated]
##     R_G_boat: not applicable (shore fit)
##   shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept:
##     R_G:      1.28 gear per crabber (1.25-1.32) [estimated]
##     R_G_boat: not applicable (shore fit)
##   private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept:
##     R_G:      1.29 gear per crabber (0.71-2.39) [fixed at 1.30]
##     R_G_boat: 3.56 gear per boat group (3.23-3.88)
# ===========================================================================
# 7.17 PE VS BSS COMPARISON TABLE
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n PE VS BSS COMPARISON (by component)\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  PE VS BSS COMPARISON (by component)
##  ============================================================
comparison <- list()
for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label_cg <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name, "_Dungeness_Kept")
    pe_label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    b <- bss_all[[label_cg]]

    pe_catch <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0
    pe_effort <- pe_all[[pe_label]]$effort_total %||% 0

    if(!is.null(b) && (is.null(b$pe_fallback) || !b$pe_fallback)) {
      bss_catch <- b$C_sum["50%"]
      bss_effort <- b$E_sum["50%"]
      catch_diff_pct <- round((bss_catch - pe_catch) / pe_catch * 100, 1)
      effort_diff_pct <- round((bss_effort - pe_effort) / pe_effort * 100, 1)

      conv <- convergence_df |> filter(fit == label_cg)
      method <- if(nrow(conv) > 0 && conv$pass_convergence[1]) "BSS" else "PE"
    } else {
      bss_catch <- NA; bss_effort <- NA
      catch_diff_pct <- NA; effort_diff_pct <- NA
      method <- "PE"
    }

    comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
      component = paste0(pop, " (", ss$name, ")"),
      PE_effort = round(pe_effort),
      BSS_effort = round(bss_effort),
      effort_diff_pct = effort_diff_pct,
      PE_catch = round(pe_catch),
      BSS_catch = round(bss_catch),
      catch_diff_pct = catch_diff_pct,
      method_selected = method
    )
  }
}

comparison[[length(comparison)+1]] <- tibble(
  component = "comm_charter (census)",
  PE_effort = round(pe_all$comm_charter$effort_total),
  BSS_effort = NA, effort_diff_pct = NA,
  PE_catch = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept),
  BSS_catch = NA, catch_diff_pct = NA,
  method_selected = "Census"
)

comparison_df <- bind_rows(comparison)
cat("\n")
print(comparison_df)
## # A tibble: 5 × 8
##   component                    PE_effort BSS_effort effort_diff_pct PE_catch BSS_catch catch_diff_pct method_selected
##   <chr>                            <dbl>      <dbl>           <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>          <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 shore (ring_net_only)            23351      10984           -53      13403      6803          -49.2 PE             
## 2 shore (all_gear)                116220      53752           -53.7    36578     19694          -46.2 PE             
## 3 private_boat (ring_net_only)      1687         NA            NA        945        NA           NA   PE             
## 4 private_boat (all_gear)          34668     283355           717.     11162    155038         1289   BSS            
## 5 comm_charter (census)              283         NA            NA      12007        NA           NA   Census
write.csv(comparison_df, file.path(output_dir, "pe_vs_bss_comparison.csv"), row.names = FALSE)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.18 SEASON SUMMARY ONE-PAGER
# ===========================================================================

cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n SEASON SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  SEASON SUMMARY
##  ============================================================
port_total_cg <- "Dungeness_Kept"
pt_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("port_total_", port_total_cg, ".csv"))
if(file.exists(pt_file)) {
  port_total <- read.csv(pt_file)
} else {
  port_total <- tibble(Estimate = c("Effort","Catch"), PE = NA, BSS_median = NA, BSS_lo95 = NA, BSS_hi95 = NA)
}

mode_catch <- c(
  Shore = round((pe_all$shore_ring_net_only$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0) + (pe_all$shore_all_gear$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)),
  `Private Boat` = round((pe_all$private_boat_ring_net_only$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0) + (pe_all$private_boat_all_gear$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)),
  `Commercial/Charter` = round(pe_all$comm_charter$Dungeness_Kept %||% 0)
)
mode_pct <- round(mode_catch / sum(mode_catch) * 100, 1)

monthly_file <- file.path(output_dir, "monthly_estimates.csv")
top_months <- if(file.exists(monthly_file)) {
  read.csv(monthly_file) |>
    arrange(desc(BSS_Catch_median)) |>
    head(5) |>
    mutate(label = sprintf("%s: %s crab", Month, format(BSS_Catch_median, big.mark=","))) |>
    pull(label)
} else { "Monthly estimates not available" }

n_bss_fits <- sum(!sapply(bss_all, function(b) !is.null(b$pe_fallback) && b$pe_fallback))
n_pass <- sum(convergence_df$pass_convergence, na.rm=TRUE)

total_effort_days <- n_distinct(c(dwg$shore_effort$event_date, dwg$boat_effort$event_date))
total_interview_n <- nrow(dwg$interview)
season_days <- as.integer(as.Date(params$est_date_end) - as.Date(params$est_date_start) + 1)

cat("\n  ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
## 
##   ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
cat("  ║         SEASON SUMMARY: 2024-25 GRAYS HARBOR        ║\n")
##   ║         SEASON SUMMARY: 2024-25 GRAYS HARBOR        ║
cat("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
##   ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
cat(sprintf("  ║  Season: %s to %s (%d days)   ║\n",
            params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end, season_days))
##   ║  Season: 2024-09-16 to 2025-09-15 (365 days)   ║
cat("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
##   ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
cat("  ║  PORT TOTAL (Dungeness Kept)                        ║\n")
##   ║  PORT TOTAL (Dungeness Kept)                        ║
if(nrow(port_total) >= 2) {
  cat(sprintf("  ║    PE:  %s crab                              ║\n",
              format(port_total$PE[2], big.mark=",")))
  if(!is.na(port_total$BSS_median[2])) {
    cat(sprintf("  ║    BSS: %s crab (%s - %s)        ║\n",
                format(port_total$BSS_median[2], big.mark=","),
                format(port_total$BSS_lo95[2], big.mark=","),
                format(port_total$BSS_hi95[2], big.mark=",")))
  }
}
##   ║    PE:  74,095 crab                              ║
##   ║    BSS: 217,971 crab (163,649 - 313,836)        ║
cat("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
##   ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
cat("  ║  HARVEST BY MODE                                    ║\n")
##   ║  HARVEST BY MODE                                    ║
for(nm in names(mode_catch)) {
  cat(sprintf("  ║    %-20s %6s (%4.1f%%)             ║\n",
              nm, format(mode_catch[nm], big.mark=","), mode_pct[nm]))
}
##   ║    Shore                49,981 (67.5%)             ║
##   ║    Private Boat         12,107 (16.3%)             ║
##   ║    Commercial/Charter   12,007 (16.2%)             ║
cat("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
##   ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
cat("  ║  TOP HARVEST MONTHS                                 ║\n")
##   ║  TOP HARVEST MONTHS                                 ║
for(m in top_months[1:min(5, length(top_months))]) {
  cat(sprintf("  ║    %s\n", m))
}
##   ║    2025-08: 54,985 crab
##   ║    2025-07: 41,839 crab
##   ║    2025-09: 21,807 crab
##   ║    2025-05: 19,801 crab
##   ║    2024-12: 15,555 crab
cat("  ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣\n")
##   ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
cat("  ║  DATA QUALITY                                       ║\n")
##   ║  DATA QUALITY                                       ║
cat(sprintf("  ║    Effort sampling: %d of %d days (%d%%)            ║\n",
            total_effort_days, season_days, round(total_effort_days/season_days*100)))
##   ║    Effort sampling: 200 of 365 days (55%)            ║
cat(sprintf("  ║    Interviews: %s total                        ║\n",
            format(total_interview_n, big.mark=",")))
##   ║    Interviews: 3,925 total                        ║
cat(sprintf("  ║    BSS convergence: %d of %d fits passed          ║\n",
            n_pass, n_bss_fits))
##   ║    BSS convergence: 1 of 3 fits passed          ║
cat("  ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n")
##   ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
season_summary <- tibble(
  metric = c("Season start", "Season end", "Season days",
             "PE total catch", "BSS total catch (median)", "BSS 95% CI lower", "BSS 95% CI upper",
             paste("Catch:", names(mode_catch)), paste("Catch %:", names(mode_catch)),
             "Effort days sampled", "Total interviews",
             "BSS fits attempted", "BSS fits passed convergence"),
  value = c(params$est_date_start, params$est_date_end, as.character(season_days),
            if(nrow(port_total)>=2) as.character(c(port_total$PE[2], port_total$BSS_median[2],
              port_total$BSS_lo95[2], port_total$BSS_hi95[2])) else rep(NA, 4),
            as.character(mode_catch), as.character(mode_pct),
            as.character(total_effort_days), as.character(total_interview_n),
            as.character(n_bss_fits), as.character(n_pass))
)
write.csv(season_summary, file.path(output_dir, "season_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n  New output tables saved.\n")
## 
##   New output tables saved.
# ===========================================================================
# 7.19 SENSITIVITY: INCOMPLETE TRIP EFFECT ON PE ESTIMATES
#
# v5.2: Compares PE estimates with and without the incomplete trip filter
#        to quantify the harvest impact. Reports per-population, per-sub-season,
#        and per-gear-type CPUE differences.
# ===========================================================================

timer_start("7_sensitivity")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n SENSITIVITY: INCOMPLETE TRIP EFFECT\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  SENSITIVITY: INCOMPLETE TRIP EFFECT
##  ============================================================
sensitivity_results <- list()

for(pop in c("shore","private_boat")) {
  for(ss in list(subseason_1, subseason_2)) {
    label <- paste0(pop, "_", ss$name)
    days_ss <- prep_days_crab(ss$start, ss$end, params$days_wkend, crabbing_holiday_dates,
                              params$period_pe, params$sections, L_eff_model)
    summ_ss <- prep_population_summary(dwg, pop, ss$start, ss$end, params)

    # Count trip status
    n_total_int <- nrow(summ_ss$interview)
    n_complete <- sum(summ_ss$interview$trip_status == "Complete", na.rm=TRUE)
    n_incomplete <- sum(summ_ss$interview$trip_status == "Incomplete", na.rm=TRUE)
    n_missing_ts <- sum(is.na(summ_ss$interview$trip_status))

    # PE with all trips
    params_all <- params; params_all$filter_incomplete_trips <- FALSE
    pe_all_trips <- run_pe(summ_ss, days_ss, params_all, paste0(label, " [all trips]"))

    # PE with completed trips only
    params_comp <- params; params_comp$filter_incomplete_trips <- TRUE
    summ_comp <- summ_ss
    summ_comp$interview <- summ_ss$interview |>
      filter(trip_status == "Complete" | is.na(trip_status))
    pe_complete <- run_pe(summ_comp, days_ss, params_comp, paste0(label, " [complete only]"))

    diff_pct <- if(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept > 0) {
      (pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept - pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept) /
        pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept * 100
    } else { NA }

    cat(sprintf("  %s: All=%s, Complete=%s (diff=%+.1f%%)\n",
                label,
                format(round(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept), big.mark=","),
                format(round(pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept), big.mark=","),
                diff_pct))

    sensitivity_results[[length(sensitivity_results)+1]] <- tibble(
      component = label,
      n_interviews_total = n_total_int,
      n_complete = n_complete,
      n_incomplete = n_incomplete,
      n_missing_status = n_missing_ts,
      pct_incomplete = round(n_incomplete / max(n_complete + n_incomplete, 1) * 100, 1),
      PE_catch_all_trips = round(pe_all_trips$Dungeness_Kept),
      PE_catch_complete_only = round(pe_complete$Dungeness_Kept),
      diff_pct = round(diff_pct, 1),
      filter_applied = isTRUE(params$filter_incomplete_trips)
    )
  }
}
## 
##   shore [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 52 effort obs, 856 interviews, crab/gear=0.76
##   PE shore_ring_net_only [all trips]: Effort=23,351 hrs, Dung=12,645
##   PE shore_ring_net_only [complete only]: Effort=23,351 hrs, Dung=13,403
##   shore_ring_net_only: All=12,645, Complete=13,403 (diff=+6.0%)
## 
##   shore [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 313 effort obs, 2743 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   PE shore_all_gear [all trips]: Effort=116,220 hrs, Dung=33,617
##   PE shore_all_gear [complete only]: Effort=116,220 hrs, Dung=36,578
##   shore_all_gear: All=33,617, Complete=36,578 (diff=+8.8%)
## 
##   private_boat [2024-09-16 to 2024-11-30]: 44 effort obs, 17 interviews, crab/gear=0.79
##   PE private_boat_ring_net_only [all trips]: Effort=1,687 hrs, Dung=945
##   PE private_boat_ring_net_only [complete only]: Effort=1,687 hrs, Dung=945
##   private_boat_ring_net_only: All=945, Complete=945 (diff=+0.0%)
## 
##   private_boat [2024-12-01 to 2025-09-15]: 196 effort obs, 145 interviews, crab/gear=0.82
##   PE private_boat_all_gear [all trips]: Effort=34,668 hrs, Dung=11,524
##   PE private_boat_all_gear [complete only]: Effort=34,668 hrs, Dung=11,162
##   private_boat_all_gear: All=11,524, Complete=11,162 (diff=-3.1%)
sensitivity_df <- bind_rows(sensitivity_results)
cat("\n")
print(sensitivity_df)
## # A tibble: 4 × 10
##   component              n_interviews_total n_complete n_incomplete n_missing_status pct_incomplete PE_catch_all_trips PE_catch_complete_only diff_pct
##   <chr>                               <int>      <int>        <int>            <int>          <dbl>              <dbl>                  <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 shore_ring_net_only                   856        627          229                0           26.8              12645                  13403      6  
## 2 shore_all_gear                       2743       1647         1091                5           39.8              33617                  36578      8.8
## 3 private_boat_ring_net…                 17         17            0                0            0                  945                    945      0  
## 4 private_boat_all_gear                 145        131           14                0            9.7              11524                  11162     -3.1
## # ℹ 1 more variable: filter_applied <lgl>
write.csv(sensitivity_df, file.path(output_dir, "sensitivity_incomplete_trips.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Gear-type-specific CPUE comparison (shore only, all sub-seasons combined)
cat("\n--- Gear-Type CPUE: Complete vs Incomplete (Shore) ---\n")
## 
## --- Gear-Type CPUE: Complete vs Incomplete (Shore) ---
shore_int <- dwg$interview |>
  filter(population == "shore",
         between(event_date, as.Date(params$est_date_start), as.Date(params$est_date_end)),
         fishing_time_total >= 0.5) |>
  mutate(
    cpue = dungeness_kept / fishing_time_total,
    has_pot      = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bpot\\b") &
                              !str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bslip\\s*ring\\b")),
    has_ring_net = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bring\\s*net\\b")),
    has_trap     = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\b(trap|star)\\b")),
    has_snare    = as.integer(str_detect(gear_type, "(?i)\\bsnare\\b")),
    n_gear_types = pmax(has_pot + has_ring_net + has_trap + has_snare, 1L)
  )

gear_sensitivity <- list()
for(gname in c("Pot","Ring Net","Trap","Snare")) {
  gcol <- c(Pot="has_pot", `Ring Net`="has_ring_net", Trap="has_trap", Snare="has_snare")[gname]
  gdata <- shore_int |> filter(.data[[gcol]] == 1, n_gear_types == 1)  # single-gear only
  if(nrow(gdata) < 10) next
  by_status <- gdata |>
    group_by(trip_status) |>
    summarise(mean_cpue = mean(cpue), n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  cpue_c <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Complete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
  cpue_i <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Incomplete") |> pull(mean_cpue)
  n_c <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Complete") |> pull(n)
  n_i <- by_status |> filter(trip_status == "Incomplete") |> pull(n)
  if(length(cpue_c) > 0 && length(cpue_i) > 0 && cpue_c > 0) {
    bias <- (cpue_i - cpue_c) / cpue_c * 100
    cat(sprintf("  %10s: Complete=%.3f (n=%d), Incomplete=%.3f (n=%d), Bias=%+.1f%%\n",
                gname, cpue_c, n_c, cpue_i, n_i, bias))
    gear_sensitivity[[length(gear_sensitivity)+1]] <- tibble(
      gear_type = gname, cpue_complete = round(cpue_c, 3), n_complete = n_c,
      cpue_incomplete = round(cpue_i, 3), n_incomplete = n_i,
      bias_pct = round(bias, 1)
    )
  }
}
##          Pot: Complete=0.298 (n=386), Incomplete=0.235 (n=261), Bias=-21.0%
##     Ring Net: Complete=0.380 (n=422), Incomplete=0.396 (n=228), Bias=+4.4%
##         Trap: Complete=0.733 (n=583), Incomplete=0.563 (n=314), Bias=-23.2%
##        Snare: Complete=0.440 (n=194), Incomplete=0.353 (n=98), Bias=-19.9%
if(length(gear_sensitivity) > 0) {
  gear_sens_df <- bind_rows(gear_sensitivity)
  write.csv(gear_sens_df, file.path(output_dir, "sensitivity_incomplete_by_gear.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}
timer_stop("7_sensitivity")
##   [TIMER] 7_sensitivity: 0.0 min
# ---- 7.20 SAVE PARAMETERS ----
timer_stop("6_outputs")
##   [TIMER] 6_outputs: 0.7 min
writeLines(capture.output(str(params)), file.path(output_dir, "run_parameters.txt"))

cat(sprintf("\nAll outputs saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
## 
## All outputs saved to: C:/Users/mattg/Desktop/github/FWC-estimation-method/05_output/20260709/gear-type-CPUE-model
cat(paste(" ", list.files(output_dir), collapse="\n"), "\n")

# ---- TIMING SUMMARY ----
run_total <- difftime(Sys.time(), run_start_time, units = "mins")
cat("\n", strrep("=",60), "\n TIMING SUMMARY\n", strrep("=",60), "\n")
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##  TIMING SUMMARY
##  ============================================================
timing_df <- tibble(
  Section = names(timer_log),
  Minutes = sapply(timer_log, function(x) round(as.numeric(x$elapsed), 1))
)
print(timing_df, n = 20)
## # A tibble: 10 × 2
##    Section                                  Minutes
##    <chr>                                      <dbl>
##  1 1_load_data                                  0  
##  2 2_diagnostic_plots                           0.1
##  3 3_PE_estimates                               0  
##  4 4_commercial_charter                         0  
##  5 5_BSS_model_runs                            35.3
##  6 BSS_shore_ring_net_only_Dungeness_Kept      10.7
##  7 BSS_shore_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept           19.8
##  8 BSS_private_boat_all_gear_Dungeness_Kept     4  
##  9 6_outputs                                    0.7
## 10 7_sensitivity                                0
cat(sprintf("\n  TOTAL RUNTIME: %.1f minutes (%.1f hours)\n",
            as.numeric(run_total), as.numeric(run_total)/60))
## 
##   TOTAL RUNTIME: 36.2 minutes (0.6 hours)
